issue_title: [Python 3.3] TypeError with buffer_class = irc.buffer.LineBuffer
issue_body:
Hi,

With python 3.3, when using `irc.client.ServerConnection.buffer_class = irc.buffer.LineBuffer`, I get the following traceback:
```
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "testbot.py", line 120, in <module>
    main()
  File "testbot.py", line 117, in main
    bot.start()
  File "/usr/pkg/lib/python3.3/site-packages/irc/bot.py", line 266, in start
    super(SingleServerIRCBot, self).start()
  File "/usr/pkg/lib/python3.3/site-packages/irc/client.py", line 1225, in start
    self.ircobj.process_forever()
  File "/usr/pkg/lib/python3.3/site-packages/irc/client.py", line 267, in process_forever
    self.process_once(timeout)
  File "/usr/pkg/lib/python3.3/site-packages/irc/client.py", line 248, in process_once
    self.process_data(i)
  File "/usr/pkg/lib/python3.3/site-packages/irc/client.py", line 213, in process_data
    c.process_data()
  File "/usr/pkg/lib/python3.3/site-packages/irc/client.py", line 561, in process_data
    self._process_line(line)
  File "/usr/pkg/lib/python3.3/site-packages/irc/client.py", line 572, in _process_line
    m = _rfc_1459_command_regexp.match(line)
TypeError: can't use a string pattern on a bytes-like object
```
This is the `testbot.py` code with the following added before `irc.bot.SingleServerIRCBot`:
`irc.client.ServerConnection.buffer_class = irc.buffer.LineBuffer`
This works with python 2.7.

----------------------------------------
- Bitbucket: https://bitbucket.org/jaraco/irc/issue/37
- Originally reported by: Emile Heitor
- Originally created at: 2014-04-20T17:51:55.350

issue_title: IP地址的問題
Missing syntax highlighting on longlist
issue_body:
`list` prints the nearby sourcecode along with syntax highlighting.

`longlist` prints the sourcecode for the entire function/method, but omits syntax highlighting.

`longlist` should have the same syntax highlighting as `list`.

issue_title: 400 from google oauth (when oauth isn't configured)
issue_body:
I'm running flower on heroku, pulling directly from master.  When I go to the app, after entering basic auth credentials, I get a google error page at `https://accounts.google.com/o/oauth2/auth?approval_prompt=auto&scope=profile+email&redirect_uri=None&response_type=code&client_id=None`

With the text
```
Error: invalid_request
Invalid parameter value for redirect_uri: Missing scheme: None
```

I'm starting flower with the following command:
`flower --port=$PORT --broker=$BROKER_URL --basic_auth=$FLOWER_AUTH --persistent --db=/tmp/flower`

$BROKER_URL points to a redis instance.

I'm not interested in using oauth.  How can I disable it?

issue_title: error C4996: 'strncpy' unsafe
issue_body:
Hi!

Today I generated a glad.c file and includes for C++ and OpenGL 4.4 using your webservice.

When I tried to include it in my C++ Project (#include as well as adding the glad.c file to my project) and building that, I got the following two errors:

`e:\files\opengl\glad.c(173): error C4996: 'strncpy': This function or variable may be unsafe. Consider using strncpy_s instead. To disable deprecation, use _CRT_SECURE_NO_WARNINGS. See online help for details.`

`c:\program files (x86)\windows kits\10\include\10.0.16299.0\ucrt\string.h(343): note: see declaration of 'strncpy'`

I tried reinstalling this exact windows kit but that didn't help.


Is there any way I can solve this problem? Or did i make a mistake when generating the files / integrating them into my project?

~ Incendor

issue_title: Typo in policy rule name
issue_body: The name of the policy rule `ProhibitImplicitScopeBuiltinVariable` is actually [implemented](https://github.com/Kuniwak/vint/blob/master/vint/linting/policy/prohibit_implicit_scope_builtin_variable.py#L12-L14) as `ProhibitImplicitScopeBuitlinVariable` (`t` and `l` in `Builtin` are swapped).  Consequently, enabling `ProhibitImplicitScopeBuiltinVariable` in `.vintrc.yaml` has no effect, the misspelled has to be used instead. :smile:

issue_title: Line/col is wrong with parse error in map function
issue_body:
Given the following file, the error should be reported somewhere in line 2, but is not:
```vim
" line 1
call map(foo, "extend(bar, {'auto_enabled': 0}, 'error'")
```

It seems to refer to the end of the string being eval'd:
```
t-syntaxerror-wrong-linecol.vim:1:46: unexpected token: <EOL> (see ynkdir/vim-vimlparser)
```

The exception is

> VimLParserException('vimlparser: unexpected token: <EOL>: line 1 col 46',)

issue_title: Inconsistent Travis issues with certain unit tests
issue_body:
Once in a while, e.g., every 10th time, Travis seems to complain about a certain unit test of the multi-layer perceptron. I couldn't find out what causes the problem but can't reproduce the issue on my local machine. Also, it works just fine via Travis ~90% of the time. I am wondering if it is related to the random state being set incorrectly, but since the random state is set equally for all classifiers inside the fit method that is imported from `_BaseSupervisedEstimator`, I don't think this is the issue here. I think it is something hardware specific maybe. 

I was wondering if someone has an idea of what could be going on here? Would really appreciate any ideas and feedback!

As a current work around this problem, I changed the unit test to the following:


```python
def test_multiclass_gd_acc():
    mlp = MLP(epochs=20,
              eta=0.05,
              hidden_layers=[10],
              minibatches=1,
              random_seed=1)
    mlp.fit(X, y)
    assert round(mlp.cost_[0], 2) == 0.55, mlp.cost_[0]

    if round(mlp.cost_[-1], 2) == 0.25:
        warnings.warn('About 10% of the time, mlp.cost_[-1] is'
                      ' 0.247213137424 when tested via Travis CI.'
                      ' Likely, it is an architecture-related problem but'
                      ' should be looked into in future.')
    else:
        assert round(mlp.cost_[-1], 2) == 0.01, mlp.cost_[-1]
        assert (y == mlp.predict(X)).all()
```

issue_body:
While init Tabu with both 'to' and 'row_height', generate_pdf gives the following exception:

>   File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/pylatex/table.py", line 493, in dumps
    raise TableError(u"Can't apply preamble to Tabu table "
pylatex.errors.TableError: Can't apply preamble to Tabu table (unexpected initial command sequence)

Setting breakpoint at line 493 of pylatex/table.py
the `_s `value is:
> u'\\renewcommand{\\arraystretch}{2.0}%\n\\begin{tabu}{X[l] X[r]}%\nProv&Num\\\\%\n\\hline%\n&\\\\%\nPA&9\\\\%\nPA&9\\\\%\nPA&9\\\\%\nPA&9\\\\%\nPA&9\\\\%\n\\end{tabu}'

`_s.preamble` value is
> u'\\renewcomman'

The source checks `_s.startswith(ur"\begin{longtabu}")` or `_s.startswith(ur"\begin{tabu}")` seems not covering the case of having `row_height` set. 

Sample source code:
```
# -*- coding: utf-8 -*-
from pylatex import Document, Command, Tabu
from pylatex.utils import NoEscape

def generage_books():
    doc = Document()
    doc.preamble.append(Command('title', NoEscape('title')))
    doc.preamble.append(Command('author', NoEscape('author')))
    doc.preamble.append(Command('date', NoEscape(r'\today')))
    doc.append(NoEscape(r'\maketitle'))

    fmt = "X[l] X[r]"
    with doc.create(Tabu(fmt, to="4in", row_height=2.0)) as data_table:
        header_row1 = ["Prov", "Num"]
        data_table.add_row(header_row1)
        data_table.add_hline()
        data_table.add_empty_row()
        row = ["PA", "9"]
        for i in range(5):
            data_table.add_row(row)

    doc.generate_pdf('book', clean_tex=False)


if __name__ == '__main__':
    generage_books()
```

issue_title: Normalize action frame initializations
issue_body:
An action's "action_counter" is an important number, to know what frame of an animation someone is in. Unfortunately, Melee is inconsistent on whether this counter starts at 0 or at 1. It depends on what animation it is. 

Let's normalize this and make it so that every animation starts at frame 1. This is already (mostly) done in the C++ version of SmashBot here, but it needs to be ported in. 

https://github.com/altf4/SmashBot/blob/master/Util/GameState.cpp#L1057

I know it's a little heretical to make something index from 1, but it makes our numbers make more intuitive sense. Don't think of this number as an index at all, in fact, but rather a counter. For instance, you may want to know when the hitbox of a particular attack first shows up. Suppose it's Fox's upsmash, which happens on frame 7. You'll do something like:

```
if opponent_state.action_frame == 7:
    #do something here
```

issue_title: StandardInput instance has no attribute 'encoding'
issue_body:
using this package in a 3d application where the python version 2.7.6

i get the error:  StandardInput instance has no attribute 'encoding'
**ldap3/utils/config.py:94**

ive modified the if statement as follows

#### FROM:

```python
if stdin and stdin.encoding:
    _DEFAULT_ENCODING = stdin.encoding
elif getdefaultencoding():
    _DEFAULT_ENCODING = getdefaultencoding()
else:
    _DEFAULT_ENCODING = 'utf-8'
```

#### TO:

```python
if stdin and (stdin.encoding if hasattr(stdin, "encoding") else False):
    _DEFAULT_ENCODING = stdin.encoding
elif getdefaultencoding():
    _DEFAULT_ENCODING = getdefaultencoding()
else:
    _DEFAULT_ENCODING = 'utf-8'
```

issue_title: SVM threaded cross validation trainer return type inconsistent and fails to compile
issue_body:
The return value of cross_validate_trainer is 
`const matrix<double, 1, 2, typename trainer_type::mem_manager_type>`

The return value of cross_validate_trainer_threaded is
`const matrix<typename trainer_type::scalar_type, 1, 2, typename trainer_type::mem_manager_type>`

Note that the value type of the matrix switched from `double` to `trainer_type::scalar_type`.  This is confusing as I expected the threaded version to be a drop-in replacement for the unthreaded version with the exception of the number of threads argument.  

Even worse, the threaded version fails to compile if `trainer_type::scalar_type` is not `double`.  I do not mind the return type being double even if `trainer_type::scalar_type` is something else (like `float`), but it should compile.  The error is a compile time assert from matrix/matrix.h line 1474 that the assignment has incompatible types.  The error message references svm/svm_threaded.h line 60.  

I think the issues is that `cvtti_helpers::task::operator()` is instantiated with `matrix_type` = `matrix<trainer_type::scalar_type, 1, 2, trainer_type::mem_manager_type>`.  See svm/svm_threaded.h lines 55, 140, and 211.  Unfortunately, the return type of `test_binary_decision_function` is `const matrix<double,1,2> test_binary_decision_function`.  This causes an assignment  at svm/svm_threaded.h line 60 from a `matrix<double,1,2>` to a `matrix<trainer_type::scalar_type,1,2>`.  

It would see like changing the return type of cross_validate_trainer_threaded to `matrix<double,1,2>` and making the corresponding changes to cvtti_helpers::task would solve both issues.  

https://github.com/davisking/dlib/blob/38a2846ddad5a81fab9905b76d061ad473001eba/dlib/svm/svm.h#L536-L547

https://github.com/davisking/dlib/blob/38a2846ddad5a81fab9905b76d061ad473001eba/dlib/svm/svm_threaded.h#L227-L239

https://github.com/davisking/dlib/blob/38a2846ddad5a81fab9905b76d061ad473001eba/dlib/matrix/matrix.h#L1472-L1475

https://github.com/davisking/dlib/blob/38a2846ddad5a81fab9905b76d061ad473001eba/dlib/svm/svm_threaded.h#L48-L60

https://github.com/davisking/dlib/blob/38a2846ddad5a81fab9905b76d061ad473001eba/dlib/svm/svm_threaded.h#L140

https://github.com/davisking/dlib/blob/38a2846ddad5a81fab9905b76d061ad473001eba/dlib/svm/svm.h#L160-L169

issue_title: Signed Distance produces zero result on certain edge cases.
issue_body:
REPRODUCEABLE CODE
```
#cuboid is a trimesh object representing a cube, with correct normals, and bounds as follows
print(cuboid.bounds) # >>> [[0,0,0],[2,2,2]]
points = np.array([[3,2,2]]) #should be 1 mm away
ret = trimesh.proximity.signed_distance(cuboid, points)
assert np.allclose(ret, [-1])  # should be true, but is false. ret ends up being [0]
```

REASON FOR FAILURE:
in trimesh.proximity.signed_distance, the "closest" triangle to the point is found, and its normal is used to get the sign. If this closest triangle is actually in the same plane as the point, then the found sign is 0. This is okay if the point is "on" the triangle, but consider the case wherein a cuboid is sitting on a table, and the point is a point somewhere on the flat surface of the table, but not under the cuboid. The current closest face / point algorithm could choose one of the triangles defining the part of the cuboid that is sitting face down on the table. In this case, even though the distance from the point is not zero, the signed distance will be returned as zero.

I'm not sure I stated this clearly, but the above test case, if run, will make sense of it.

```
a = Trimesh(**other_mesh.to_dict())
```

```
new_mesh = Trimesh(**d)
```

Regards!

issue_title: warning "Failed to parse ClientStateUpdate:..." raised when leaving a group chat
issue_body:
This warning is logged when leaving a group chat:
```
Failed to parse ClientStateUpdate: Message field 'client_conversation': Message field 'self_conversation_state': Message field 'self_read_state': Message is not optional
```

It does not seem to visibly affect anything else - after dumping the payloads being processed by parser.py - I note that the stated fields in the payload are all empty.

I tried tweaking schemas.py to make the fields `is_optional` and succeeded in suppressing the warning. Unfortunately, **this introduced a worse error** when leaving a conversation: the hangups library first crashes with an exception on `latest_read_timestamp`, then on each subsequent reconnection, crashes with an exception in `conversation.py:add_conversation`

I leave my experimental changes for reference purposes in this commit: https://github.com/endofline/hangups/commit/68eb8c37ae850f84d80fd943ad25165efc98323d

It is not recommended to merge this due to the above error. I suspect by suppressing the original warning, I allowed an incomplete payload to be parsed and used by the library.

issue_title: Feature request: Alerts, reset based on actual time of snooze
issue_body:
When snoozing an alert dialog for x minutes, it seems to snooze from original scheduled time (not desireable), as opposed to actual time of snooze (desireable).

IOW, one has an alert triggered at 2pm ("@a 0:m"), and snoozes it for 10 minutes, but lets more time go by than that value, e.g. 15 minutes, before addressing this pop-up.

The alert dialog box (the pop-up) which appeared at 2:10 PM stays on the screen with the 10 minutes snooze value "selected", waiting to be addressed (desireable/normal behavior).

If at that point--15 minutes from original time--one just clicks "Repeat" and leaves the same value of 10 minutes, the next Alert pops-up NOT 10 minutes from **that** (current) time--which it should--but 5 minutes based on the _last_ (past) snooze time,  This creates an undesireable offset which can cause the Alert to pop-up again, almost instantly in some cases, in any case much before the chosen snooze time (e.g. 10 minutes).  The user, whom is under the impression he/she just snoozed for 10 minutes, is presented with the Alert dialog at shorter intervals than 10 minutes, having to Snooze again, more times than necessary.  This is undesireable.

The Snooze value should reset from the time of the actual Snooze, so that if one snoozes for 15 minutes at 3:12 PM, NO further Alerts pops up until 3: 27 PM , no matter what.

issue_title: For counter with n registers, it is possible to set the n+1'th register
Issues with function keys+modifiers
issue_body:
I recently wrote a utility using this library that's meant to integrate with twitch. To try and not interfere with other keybinds, I was using the F13-24 keys with modifiers with a custom keyboard. However, this library seems to not really handle these well at all.

Test:
```python
import keyboard
tests = ["f11", "f12", "shift+f11", "shift+f12"]
for key in tests:
    keyboard.add_hotkey(key, lambda: print(key))
keyboard.wait()
```

Expected result:
Pressing f12 should trigger the f12 hook, pressing shift+f12 triggers the shift+f12 hook
Actual result:
Pressing f11 or f12 triggers the shift+f12 hook.

It seems to just trigger the last keybind that was added in further testing. Am I doing something wrong?

All this was run on Windows 10 using Python 2.7.14.

issue_title: Docstrings in pattern functions need more detail
issue_body:
The documentation for `patterns.evaluate` and `io.load_patterns` does not actually explain the data structure produced by the loader, or required by the evaluator.

In `load_patterns`, you get
```
Returns
-------
pattern_list : list
    The list of patterns, containing all their occurrences,
    using the following format::

        pattern_list = [pattern1, ..., patternN]
        pattern = [occurrence1, ..., occurrenceM]
        occurrence = [onset_midi1, ..., onset_midiO]
        onset_midi = (onset_time, midi_number)

    E.g.::
        P = [[[(77.0, 67.0), (77.5, 77.0), ... ]]]
```
but this does not explain what `N`, `M`, and `O` are.

Hey guys,

I wanted to install the py3 branch (it seems to now have installed properly) and I think I
discovered a little issue:

I run test.sh first and then tried "setup.py install" which stopped with an error:

"error: can't copy 'pybedtools/test/data/__pycache__': doesn't exist or not a regular file"

Hope this helps :]

issue_title: Issue with parser cache and (nested?) namespace packages
issue_body:
I've noticed that Jedi fails to find definitions in a particular setup, which is related to cached parsers.
It looks like something gets cached in a wrong way (and `jedi.cache.clear_time_caches(True)` fixes it).

```
% virtualenv venv-django_filter
% source venv-django_filter/bin/activate
% pip install django_filter==0.15
% python django_filter.py
```

```python
import jedi
# import inspect
# print(inspect.getfile(jedi))

jedi.settings.use_filesystem_cache = False

source = """import django_filters
django_filters.rest_framework.filters.OrderingFilter()
"""

print("=== without path ===")
script = jedi.Script(source, column=42, line=2)
d1 = script.goto_definitions()
assert len(d1)
print("=== without path again ===")
script = jedi.Script(source, column=42, line=2)
d2 = script.goto_definitions()
assert len(d2)
```

I've bisected this and found that with the following commit the following assertion fails already (also in v0.9.0):

commit 8fca3f7 (HEAD, refs/bisect/bad)
Date:   Tue Apr 14 17:36:20 2015 +0200

    Add a py__name__ call to modules. This makes listing the qualified names of modules possible (in combination with the module_name_cache). Fixes #519.

`django_filters/rest_framework/filters.py` ([source](https://github.com/carltongibson/django-filter/blob/73b2228d777d9d97ed622b9b14a6110bf5ba4c7f/django_filters/rest_framework/filters.py)) uses:
```python
from ..filters import *
```

This was changed in the following commit.  Since then the 2nd assertion fails:

commit ec51891 (refs/bisect/bad)
Date:   Sat Jul 30 02:44:09 2016 +0200

    Fix nested namespace packages. At least now there's no error anymore. Fixes #743.

issue_title: HiDPI support on Mac OS X and PyQt5/(4)
issue_body:
Hi!

I have noticed that diagrams drawn on a MacBook with retina display is shown in the lower left corner of the window at a quarter of the size. This is apparently related to QGLWidget using actual device pixels. 

I am wondering if this is known or you need a helping hand fixing this if yes what is the best approach?

I did a quick hack by modifying Figure class in qtcommon.py:

    def __init__(self, parent, *args, **kwargs):
        
        # keep same documentation
        self.__doc__ = BaseFigure.__doc__
        self._widget = None
        self._widget_args = (parent, args, kwargs)
        if kwargs.get('create_widget', True):
            self.CreateWidget()

        # Determine pixel ratio - JL

        self.pixelRatio = int(self._widget.windowHandle().devicePixelRatio())            
        
        # call original init AFTER we created the widget
        BaseFigure.__init__(self)

and multiplying width and height with this constant in:

    def _GetPosition(self):
        """ Get the position of the widget. """
        if self._widget and not self._destroyed:
            tmp = self._widget.geometry()
            return tmp.left(), tmp.top(), tmp.width()*self.pixelRatio, tmp.height()*self.pixelRatio
        return 0, 0, 0, 0

I could try to help out and fix this, but I would like to know how it should be best implemented.

Thanks for a great library!

-- Jonas

issue_title: Running an action before a dotfile is installed
issue_body:
Hi, I'm struggling to use dotdrop for my emacs setup, I use the following configuration helper, which is required for my setup: https://github.com/bbatsov/prelude.

The installer for that helper does the following:

1. Move `~/.emacs.d/` (the relevant dotfile dir) to `~/.emacs.d.bak/`
2. Install a whole bunch of new files in `~/.emacs.d/`

Obviously this means that any dotfiles dotdrop attempts to install are overwritten immediately when it runs an install action for this.

Copying files into this directory immediately after the installer ran would solve this issue, is this possible, or would you accept a PR for this?

issue_title: accept account-key from stdin
issue_body:
People may want to trigger renew from another machine in order to avoid the account-key to stay inside the production system.
Renew may be triggered by simply calling ``cat account-key.key | ssh renew-cert.py`` but for any solution like this to work, acme-tiny must be able to get the the account-key from stdin.
``openssl rsa`` already accept ``/dev/stdin`` as a valid argument but:
* I didn't test ``openssl dgst``
* stdin can't be consumed twice or more (once per domain)

That means the program would have to store the value in a variable.

* Does such a request appear legitimate?
* Would you accept a PR for this?
* What diffstat is acceptable/unacceptable?

issue_title: Multithreading/processing KafkaProducer and KafkaConsumer
issue_body:
What is the correct way, if there is one, to run a KafkaProducer function while a KafkaConsumer function is running? Barring this, is it possible to have a KafkaConsumer read messages sent in the last few seconds so I can just run them one after another?

issue_title: supersample_factor and exp_time
issue_body:
Hi Laura,

I noticed that when the user sets an exposure time in `TransitModel`, since the supersample factor defaults to 1, there's nothing that makes it clear that both `exp_time` and `supersample_factor` need to be set in order to produce the light curve you want. I think it might be worthwhile to throw a warning or exception in this case, as it might not be obvious to someone who just skims through the documentation. I'm using commit 972164f.

issue_title: Weird behavior with new Google standards
issue_body:
Hello,

Google will be rolling out a new policy regarding TLS http://googleonlinesecurity.blogspot.fr/2015/09/disabling-sslv3-and-rc4.html 
I wanted to try sslyze with their new test https://cert-test.sandbox.google.com/ but sslyze is taking long time to process the because of timeout with TLS v1 and TLS v1.1 Do you know why it is in time-out rather than in "rejected" ?

On a side note, their certificate is rejected by the OS X trust store but accepted by others. 

Edit: regarding https://github.com/nabla-c0d3/sslyze/issues/85 I've tried with just `--tlsv1` and `--tlsv1_2` and it also triggers timeout. But `--tlsv1_2` is working spot on

issue_title: pyodbc and pylint
issue_body:
I talked to the pylint team about why it has trouble with pyodbc.  Here is the response to the question, "What can pyodbc do to help?"


Not familiar with pyodbc's code base, but from a quick glance, it seems to be a C extension. This puts pylint into a tough situation, since it needs to be able to parse the code into ASTs and use those trees for generating objects that can be internally interpreted. In this case, without having access to a source code, pylint can import the respective module and build some kind of ASTs objects on top of that. This is of course a security risk and we shouldn't do this blindly, an extension might be malformed, might take a lot of resources at import time or any other weird scenario you can imagine, hence the use of --extension-pkg-whitelist to specify to pylint that it can include your module for a deep analysis without worrying too much about security.
Now you might now like using this. As an alternative, we do support providing some hints to pylint, through https://github.com/PyCQA/astroid/tree/master/astroid/brain, in which we can say how a particular module or class can look like. It's basically like a stub, but on steroids, since you have access to the entire tree and inference API when building the stub. This might be another way of improving the situation for pyodbc for instance, by providing such a stub, through which pylint can know how the API of pyodbc looks like.
In the future, we will also support PEP 484 stubs, so that will be definitely easier and more straight forward to do.

ref: [https://github.com/PyCQA/pylint/issues/1209](url)

issue_title: Daemonize should kill the parent process more quickly
issue_body:
So Daemonize uses `sys.exit` in the parent process after the fork. The intention is likely that this will cause the parent process to immediately end.

However, this is not actually the case:
 * The parent python process raises SystemExit instead, which returns control flow outside of the Daemonize call if an exception handler for SystemExit is present. This means that code can continue to execute in the parent process after the Fork. Strange, but something a programmer can avoid with careful coding.
 * However, if the parent process has passed file descriptors of sockets/pipes/etc. to `keep_fds` that it intends for the client to use, when the parent process exits, the parent process will garbage collect many of these client objects and these garbage collection routines will then attempt to do clean-ups. These clean-ups may involve sending shutdown notices over the sockets/pipes. This corrupts the child process. We noticed this when we changed a client library to properly implement garbage collection.

I suggest that, instead of calling`sys.exit(0)`, that Daemonize reset the signal handlers to the system default handlers (away from the python default handlers that raise exceptions) and then call `os.kill` on itself with SIGTERM (or SIGKILL). This would prevent Python garbage collection routines in the parent from having the ability to interfere with the child process.

issue_title: Scrolling helper for all modules
issue_body:
If modules does not have `on_click` method, should we help them with this function?
```
    def on_click(self, event):
        # Assign buttons
        buttons = (None, 'left', 'middle', 'right', 'up', 'down')
        try:
            button = buttons[event['button']]
        except IndexError:
            return

        # Avoid hitting rate limits on APIs
        if button in ('up', 'down'):
            self.py3.prevent_refresh()
```
This method is targeted at API modules, but could work fine with all modules.

Opt 1) We could add that `on_click` method in only API modules as to ensure that nobody will make massive API calls (accidentally or intentional) due to mouse scrolling, but still allow calls via left/right clicks.

Opt 2) Scrolling helper to avoid mouse scrolling for all modules without `on_click`.

Opt 3) Nothing. Close this.

issue_title: Reduce polling for XF (SpaceBattles/SufficientVelocity/etc) sites
issue_body:
I've had a case of someone using FFF to hit the SpaceBattles some ~30000 times a day for updates.

It looks like you aren't caching HTTP redirects from the thread id to the 'canonical' url, and it looks possible that your app can send multiple simultaneous requests for the same content.

It appears to be the same content being requested in a tight loop, over an over again. SpaceBattles's loadbalancer health checks hit a vastly cheaper monitoring point less times per day.

I'm aware you can't enforce this on the user's of your software, or even force people to update, but some sane defaults or warnings about being overly aggressive would be appreciated.

issue_title: segfault w/msg queue threaded rearm under BSD
issue_body:
The test `test_request_notification_threaded_rearm()` sometimes segfaults on FreeBSD. I don't know why it should segfault, but running with DPRINTF enabled is informative. Under Linux, the DPRINTF log shows an orderly, repeated sequence of events --
 - `MessageQueue_request_notification` is called by the main thread (once)
 - `PyEval_InitThreads` is called once
 - `MessageQueue_request_notification` exits
 - `mq_send()` is called
 - A new C thread is invoked
 - The C thread invokes the Python callback
 - The callback requests notification, which results in a 'Notification cancelled' entry in the log
 - `MessageQueue_request_notification()` exits
 - 'Done calling' marks the end of the callback
 - Sometime after this point the thread exits
 - `mq_receive()` is called (by the test case)
 - `mq_send()` is called (by the test case) and the cycle repeats

Implicit in the description above is the behavior of the `Threading.Event` object which the test case relies on to awake the main thread when the callback is invoked. Once the main thread is woken, it should quickly call `mq.receive()`. (Keep in mind that notifications should only be sent when the queue transitions from empty to not-empty.)

Under FreeBSD, the sequence of events is diferent. Everything appears normal until after the first call to `mq_receive()` which is immediately followed by the first call to `mq_send()`. After that, the log shows a loop of over 500,000 iterations of callback/rearm without `mq_receive()` being called.

I'm suspicious of the code in uipc_mqueue.c marked by this comment --

	/*
	 * if there is no receivers and message queue
	 * is not empty, we should send notification
	 * as soon as possible.
	 */

When installing suds_requests from pypi with a recent version of pip, the most recent installable release is list as "0.2.post1". However, when the resulting package is installed, pip reports it as version "0.2". This is a problem with any automated tooling that needs to consume the installed version of a package.

Recommended solution: Put out a new release with a setup.py that matches the version as it appears on pypi (whatever that may be).

issue_title: Help? Using jwt_required() decorator correctly.
issue_body:
I am hoping to use flask-jwt for authentication to my RESTful API. Here is my setup:

`def authenticate(email, password):
    account = connection.Account.find_one({'email': email})

    if account and safe_str_comp(account.password.encode('utf-8'), password.encode('utf-8')):
        return account

    return None

def identity(payload):
    email = payload['identity']
    account = connection.Account.find_one({'email': email})
    return account

# configuration
app = Flask(__name__)
app.config.from_object(__name__)
app.config['DATABASE'] = 'arpgmanager'
app.config['MONGODB_HOST'] = '127.0.0.1'
app.config['MONGODB_PORT'] = 27017
app.config['SECRET_KEY'] = '[redacted]'

logging.basicConfig()

jwt = JWT(app, authenticate, identity)`

Please note that the Flask server is running in DEBUG mode.

And here is the endpoint:

`@app.route('/player/<pid>')
@jwt_required()
def get_player(pid):
    player = connection.Player.find_one({'id': pid})
    return dumps(player)`

Scenario: I call the endpoint without first calling /auth and getting an access token.
Expected: The JSON dump of the player object is protected and does not get returned.
Actual: The JSON dump of the player object is returned to the client as normal. An ERROR saying the request doesn't contain an access token is logged to the console.

I believe I'm not understand this correctly the documentation correctly. Should I, in the endpoint decorated by jwt_required, be manually checking for an error, or for some state of current_identity and determining whether to respond to the requester? I did not expect a protected endpoint to work without an access token.

Thanks in advance for any guidance you can provide?

A user was asking today a [question](https://www.reddit.com/r/flask/comments/5o5d0k/proper_way_of_handling_facebooktwitter_oauth/) on Reddit regarding the proper way to store OAuth access tokens. He uses this project, and was following the advice from this extension and its examples of storing the tokens obtained from providers in the Flask user session.

I was wondering if I'm missing something here, because those tokens will go out to clients as part of the session cookie and without any encryption, so they are potentially accessible to anyone who has access to the client computer.

issue_title: Add MAT-file interop with Julia tests
issue_body:
The [Julia](http://julialang.org/) MAT package (https://github.com/simonster/MAT.jl) provides the ability in Julia to read MAT files (v7.3 and the v5 format that SciPy can work with) and write MAT files (only the v7.3 format currently).

The people who have written the code in that package have also reverse engineered part of the Matlab v7.3 MAT file format metadata. It makes sense to write tests that use that package in Julia to check both of our implementations. It will be useful for catching regressions in the hdf5storage package.

issue_title: Problem with saving file on s3 storage. How save works?
issue_body:
Since version 1.1.9+ (ofter fork) you changed S3BotoStorageFile _file attribute from StringIO to SpooledTemporaryFile.

In my code I use PIL for generate picture thumbnail like this:

``` python
img = Image.open(default_storage.open(instance.uploaded.name, 'r'))
img.thumbnail(meta_data['size'], Image.ANTIALIAS)
f = default_storage.open(str('%s' + meta_data['prefix'] + '%s') % (file_path, filename,), 'w')
img.save(f, ext, quality=90)
f.close()
```

and weird think was that it works for png but not for jpg files.
After some investigation (https://github.com/python-pillow/Pillow/issues/1940) I found that there is two ways of saving files in Pillow

``` python
# file ImageFile.py
    try:
        fh = fp.fileno()
        fp.flush()
    except (AttributeError, io.UnsupportedOperation):
        # compress to Python file-compatible object
        for e, b, o, a in tile:
            e = Image._getencoder(im.mode, e, a, im.encoderconfig)
            if o > 0:
                fp.seek(o, 0)
            e.setimage(im.im, b)
            while True:
                l, s, d = e.encode(bufsize)
                fp.write(d)
                if s:
                    break
            if s < 0:
                raise IOError("encoder error %d when writing image file" % s)
            e.cleanup()
    else:
        # slight speedup: compress to real file object
        for e, b, o, a in tile:
            e = Image._getencoder(im.mode, e, a, im.encoderconfig)
            if o > 0:
                fp.seek(o, 0)
            e.setimage(im.im, b)
            s = e.encode_to_file(fh, bufsize)
            if s < 0:
                raise IOError("encoder error %d when writing image file" % s)
            e.cleanup()
```

First way (when error is throw) call python's file write method and second no.

It works for version 1.1.8 where was use StringIO and doesn't work for newer versions where SpooledTemporaryFile is used.

Is this bug? How to fix it?

issue_title: Configuring os.environ to set CC and CXX variables affects compilation of Cython extensions.
issue_body:
Function `pythran.config.init_cfg` sets `os.environ` which can be a mess when using Pythran and Cython extensions in tandem. Esp. when you want to use different compilers for pythran (say clang) and for cython (say intel, gcc etc). Simply `import pythran` messes the environment variables. 

The trouble is I see `pythran.config.cfg` being imported into `pythran.toolchain`, `pythran.types.types` and `pythran.dist`. 

A possible solution would be to use [contextmanager](https://gist.github.com/igniteflow/7267431#gistcomment-2553451) to configure `os.environ`. Although, I am not 100% confident that distutils will appreciate this while compiling the pythran extension.

issue_title: Suggest using PUT instead of POST when creating list members
issue_body:
There is an issue with how MailChimp handles deletion of list members, in that they don't delete them from the list, they change the status to "". If you want to add that person back to the list, you have to use the PUT operation to update the list member (PATCH apparently doesn't work).

http://developer.mailchimp.com/documentation/mailchimp/reference/lists/members/#

Since the PUT operation allows add or update, it might make sense to use that consistently instead of POST for creating a list member?

issue_title: lists.update_members requires "status" for users, when Mailchimp API does not
issue_body:
The "status" value is required by the function, but is not required by the Mailchimp API.

In my use case, I need to be able to update members' data without overwriting their status. The Mailchimp api allows the handy "status_if_new" parameter, which will update all other given fields and merge tags for a member without overwriting status.

I understand the reasoning for requiring "status" (the API call does nothing for a new user without it), but it breaks my use case. Instead, you could require either "status" or "status_if_new", or just drop the requirement entirely.

I can create a PR for either version. Which do you think is better? I think requiring just one of them is best.

issue_title: JSON callback output numbers as strings
issue_body:
Since v0.1.9, all JSON values are converted to string and get double quoted.

This can be fixed by adding `encodehelpers.bytes_to_unicode()` an 'opt-in' argument to skip numbers and using it in `JSONCallback.encode_value()`

See https://github.com/sripathikrishnan/redis-rdb-tools/issues/86#issuecomment-288060497 for an example.

issue_title: Rename and organize build module
issue_body:
Right now it looks like instead of starting out with a master plan, we started out coding and just let things evolve. Which is how it went. Which is perfectly ok. But we should now take a step back and make sure everything looks like a cohesive piece of software.

* Give the code generator module a name. nimi-pygen? nimi-python-gen?
* Get rid of cop-out generic names like helper
* Organize directories: Do we want templates inside build/ ? Do we want makefiles inside build/? Maybe?
* Rename extract_ functions to filter_*

issue_title: No need for InvalidRepeatedCapabilityError
issue_body:
I was thinking about the InvalidRepeatedCapabilityError class. I think we could remove it and simply get away with [ValueError](https://docs.python.org/2/library/exceptions.html#exceptions.ValueError).

This seems more Pythonic and lighter weight.

Alternatively, InvalidRepeatedCapabilityError should derive from ValueError, so client code can catch it.

issue_title: The changes to client.users() API has some missing test coverage
issue_body:
Unless I'm mistaken the test_users_return_the_correct_user_objects() test isn't covering calling users() without any params. Code just calling:

```
clients.user()
```

isn't covered. What this means is that someone could change the production code to some combination of this:

```
def users(self, limit=-1, offset=-1, filter_term=''):
     ...
```

And all tests would continue to pass... even though the change introduces a bug. I suggest that when params have defaults we should have test coverage verifying expected behavior when those defaults are relied upon. I wouldn't be surprised if we're missing this sort of coverage in other places. I just looked at group.membership() tests... and this sort of test is missing there as well.

issue_title: Introduce a CmdServer and turn the command oq-engine into a client
issue_body: This is preliminary to the remove postgres project. Once we have a client-server architecture in place, all interactions with the database and the datastore will be mediated by the server, so the issues of a multiple user situation will disappear. Only the `openquake` user will access the database and datastore, being `openquake` the user running the CmdServer and the Web UI. The supervisor will have to start the CmdServer too.

issue_title: Error annotations and binary annotations
issue_body:
In #13, it was mentioned that we don't have error annotations. Since the `__exit__` function in the context manager gets exception information, it should be pretty easy to add an error annotation (and an error binary annotation with exception information)

The thrift file does a better job than me at describing error annotations:
```
***** Annotation.value or BinaryAnnotation.key ******
/**
 * When an annotation value, this indicates when an error occurred. When a
 * binary annotation key, the value is a human readable message associated
 * with an error.
 *
 * Due to transient errors, an ERROR annotation should not be interpreted
 * as a span failure, even the annotation might explain additional latency.
 * Instrumentation should add the ERROR binary annotation when the operation
 * failed and couldn't be recovered.
 *
 * Here's an example: A span has an ERROR annotation, added when a WIRE_SEND
 * failed. Another WIRE_SEND succeeded, so there's no ERROR binary annotation
 * on the span because the overall operation succeeded.
 *
 * Note that RPC spans often include both client and server hosts: It is
 * possible that only one side perceived the error.
 */
const string ERROR = "error"
```

issue_title: Symbolic memory: unexpected load results
issue_body:
Hi,

I am playing with angr for a research project. Please, look at this script:
```
import angr
import simuvex
import claripy

binary = '/bin/ls' # we don't care for the binary, we just need an initial state
project = angr.Project(binary, load_options={'auto_load_libs' : False})

state = project.factory.entry_state(remove_options={simuvex.o.LAZY_SOLVES}, 
	add_options={simuvex.o.SYMBOLIC_WRITE_ADDRESSES}) # store range => 128, load range => 1024 

# an address which is in a valid region
start_addr = state.libc.heap_location 
state.libc.heap_location += 32 # mark 32 bytes as used 

assert state.se.any_int(state.memory.permissions(start_addr)) == 0x3
assert state.se.any_int(state.memory.permissions(start_addr + 1)) == 0x3
assert state.se.any_int(state.memory.permissions(start_addr + 2)) == 0x3

# initialize memory: 3 bytes starting at start_addr
state.memory.store(start_addr, claripy.BVV(0x0, 24), 3)

# a symbolic pointer that can be equal to [start_addr, start_addr + 1]
addr = claripy.BVS('addr', 64) 
state.se.add(addr >= start_addr)
state.se.add(addr <= start_addr + 1)
addrs = state.se.any_n_int(addr, 10)
assert len(addrs) == 2 and set(addrs) == set([start_addr, start_addr + 1])

val = 0xABCD

# symbolic store at addr
state.memory.store(addr, claripy.BVV(val, 16), 2)

# symbolic load at addr
res = state.memory.load(addr, 2)

# print res

res = state.se.any_n_int(res, 20) # up to 20 solutions
print "Obtained value(s): " + (' '.join(hex(x) for x in res))
print "Expected value:    " + hex(val)
 
assert len(res) == 1 and res[0] == val
```
I do not understand why I don't get what I expect (i.e., `res` should be always equal to the value of `val`). What am I missing? Is it an actual bug?

issue_title: SimulationManager Not Handling Ctrl-C
issue_body: Looks like `SimulationManager` doesn't handle Ctrl-C during execution the same way Path Groups did. Specifically, ctrl-c with SimMgr will return and empty SimMgr, whereas Path Group would retain it's paths.

issue_title: VSA .add() API inconsistent
issue_body:
We discussed this somewhat on irc some weeks ago.

This is the test code:

```python
sv = claripy.FullFrontend(claripy.backends.z3)
x = claripy.BVS('x', 32)
sv.add(x < 50000) 
sv.add(x < 30000) 
sv.add(x > 20000)
print sv.max(x),"-",sv.min(x)

sv = claripy.FullFrontend(claripy.backends.z3)
x = claripy.BVS('x', 32)
sv.add([x < 50000, x < 30000, x > 20000])
print sv.max(x),"-",sv.min(x)
```
Which yields the following (correct) results:
```
29999 - 20001
29999 - 20001
```

If I replace the solver to vsa:
```python
sv = claripy.ReplacementFrontend(claripy.LightFrontend(claripy.backends.vsa), replace_constraints=True, complex_auto_replace=True)
x = claripy.BVS('x', 32)
sv.add(x < 50000) 
sv.add(x < 30000) 
sv.add(x > 20000)
print sv.max(x),"-",sv.min(x)

sv = claripy.ReplacementFrontend(claripy.LightFrontend(claripy.backends.vsa), replace_constraints=True, complex_auto_replace=True)
x = claripy.BVS('x', 32)
sv.add([x < 50000, x < 30000, x > 20000])
print sv.max(x),"-",sv.min(x)
```

Yields the following:
```
49999 - 0
29999 - 19999
```
It seems, that VSA only uses the first specified constraint (first `add` call), when calculating its result.
[This seems inconsistent with the functionality of `add` function if the backend is set to Z3.]

If we specify all the constraints in one `add` call, then it works, but it miscalculates the UGT(>) expression (probably when inverting it).

issue_title: Minor issue in retryhandler - random.random() can return 0
issue_body:
in the function delay_exponential, at [line 53](https://github.com/boto/botocore/blob/c632931a6cc5eab4113c976d430bcb9c059f829f/botocore/retryhandler.py#L53), base is set to random.random(). However, the documentation (and the other branch of that if) declare that "Base must be greater than 0.", and the [spec for random.random()](https://docs.python.org/3/library/random.html#random.random) is that it will return a float in the range [0.0, 1.0), which includes 0.0. 

Thus, in extremely rare cases, time_to_sleep could repeatedly be 0, defeating the purpose of exponential backoff.

issue_title: Proposal ref(fleet): use multiple etcd servers
issue_body:
With the same conditions mentioned in #3844 I see this behavior in fleet:

```
...
Jun 10 18:05:16 nodo-4 fleetd[692]: INFO client.go:292: Failed getting response from http://localhost:4001/: cancelled
Jun 10 18:05:16 nodo-4 fleetd[692]: INFO client.go:292: Failed getting response from http://localhost:4001/: cancelled
Jun 10 18:05:16 nodo-4 fleetd[692]: INFO client.go:292: Failed getting response from http://localhost:4001/: cancelled
Jun 10 18:05:16 nodo-4 fleetd[692]: WARN engine.go:115: Engine completed reconciliation in 4.696940778s
...
```
(approximately every 10 minutes)
and that trigger a re-scheduling of the running units in the node.

and then
```
....
Jun 10 18:31:45 nodo-4 systemd[1]: Starting Network Service...
Jun 10 18:31:46 nodo-4 systemd-networkd[27471]: vethc2e8385     : could not find udev device: No such device
Jun 10 18:31:46 nodo-4 systemd-networkd[27471]: could not add new link: No such device
Jun 10 18:31:46 nodo-4 systemd-networkd[27471]: Could not enumerate links: Device or resource busy
Jun 10 18:31:46 nodo-4 systemd[1]: systemd-networkd.service: main process exited, code=exited, status=1/FAILURE
Jun 10 18:31:46 nodo-4 systemd[1]: Failed to start Network Service.
Jun 10 18:31:46 nodo-4 systemd[1]: Unit systemd-networkd.service entered failed state.
Jun 10 18:31:46 nodo-4 systemd[1]: systemd-networkd.service failed.
Jun 10 18:31:46 nodo-4 systemd[1]: systemd-networkd.service has no holdoff time, scheduling restart.
Jun 10 18:31:46 nodo-4 systemd[1]: Starting Network Service...
Jun 10 18:31:46 nodo-4 systemd-networkd[27474]: Enumeration completed
Jun 10 18:31:46 nodo-4 systemd[1]: Started Network Service.
Jun 10 18:31:46 nodo-4 systemd-networkd[27474]: veth78af1d4     : link configured
Jun 10 18:31:46 nodo-4 systemd-networkd[27474]: veth39a6795     : link configured
...
```

Adding more than one etcd server in the fleet unit using `FLEET_ETCD_SERVERS` removes this strange behavior

issue_title: Pipenv fails to install dependencies for pytest on CircleCI
issue_body:
I ran into this issue on CircleCI in a private repo which runs tests using `pytest`, and where `pytest` and the other dependencies are installed using `pipenv`.

Until recently this worked fine and the CircleCI build passed. However, after a push to this repo today the CirlcleCI build now fails to install the dependencies for `pytest` - when trying to run `pytest` it complains about the missing module `six`.

Strangely, I can't reproduce this issue on a local machine, but it definitely fails on CircleCI (see link to minimal repo reproducing the error below). I'm not sure if it's specific to CircleCI or simply due to some differences in the setup.

##### Describe your environment

Here is the CirleCI build environment:

1. OS Type: Debian GNU/Linux 8
2. Python version: 3.6.4
3. Pipenv version: 11.1.11

##### Expected result

```
$ pipenv run pytest --version
This is pytest version 3.4.2, imported from /home/circleci/.local/share/virtualenvs/project-zxI9dQ-Q/lib/python3.6/site-packages/pytest.py
```

##### Actual result

```
$ pipenv run pytest --version
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/home/circleci/.local/share/virtualenvs/project-zxI9dQ-Q/bin/pytest", line 7, in <module>
    from pytest import main
```

Environment: AzureCloud

Description:

There is no way to reset the `/etc/ssh/sshd_config` back to the version the Linux VM was deployed with using the pycli.  If a breaking change is made to `/etc/ssh/sshd_config` and the user is locked out from using SSH, the VM extensions need to be used to reset the SSH config.

Azure VM extensions will be able to reset the `/etc/ssh/sshd_config` file back to its state it was originally deployed with.

The pycli does not have this feature.  The user must use the node-cli to reset SSH.

issue_title: StorageClient.location_mode does not support 'PrimaryThenSecondary'
issue_body:
Currently, StorageClient.location_mode support only `PRIMARY` and `SECONDARY` values, see below
>    :ivar LocationMode location_mode:
>         The host location to use to make requests. Defaults to LocationMode.PRIMARY.
>         Note that this setting only applies to RA-GRS accounts as other account 
>         types do not allow reading from secondary. If the location_mode is set to 
>         LocationMode.SECONDARY, read requests will be sent to the secondary endpoint. 
>         Write requests will continue to be sent to primary.

However, according to MSFT docs, there must be `PrimaryThenSecondary` and `SecondaryThenPrimary` values, check https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/storage/storage-designing-ha-apps-with-ragrs 

> If you are using the storage client library to access RA-GRS data, you can specify the retry behavior of a read request by setting a value for the LocationMode property to one of the following:
> PrimaryOnly (the default)
> PrimaryThenSecondary
> SecondaryOnly
> SecondaryThenPrimary

Pls, add support for missing `PrimaryThenSecondary` and `SecondaryThenPrimary` values in Python SDK

I can see kinto.js does not work on IE10 and IE11. What is the browser support on this please?

issue_title: Write a memcache cache backend
issue_body: Memcache is a great candidate for a cache backend, because it's a key-value cache with a TTL. One of our ops guys mentioned that memcache is more robust than redis because it's not master-slave replicated.

issue_title: Implement `getRecording`
write-htcondor-job-spec.py accepts FASTQ files but should not
issue_body: The `write-htcondor-job-spec.py` command accepts a FASTQ file as input and splits it as though it were FASTA. This shouldn't be possible as the resulting "FASTA" has quality strings as sequence ids. At least we should look at the file name and give a warning if it ends in `fastq`.

issue_title: Need nb_conda for Python 3.6
issue_body:
There currently does not seem to be support for `nb_conda` that is compatible with Python 3.6.  This makes it cumbersome to work with Py36 environments that use Jupyter.  Or in Jupyter when you need to choose an environment (whichever way you want to look at it).

```bash
  - python 3.6*
Use "conda info <package>" to see the dependencies for each package.
```

issue_title: Conda 4 does respect anaconda-client url
issue_body:
```
conda --debug search --over -c org conda
Using Anaconda Cloud api site http://localhost:4990/api
DEBUG:binstar:Found login token: /Users/sean/Library/Application Support/binstar/http%3A%2F%2Flocalhost%3A4990%2Fapi.token
DEBUG:conda.fetch:channel_urls=OrderedDict([('https://conda.anaconda.org/org/osx-64/', ('org', 1)), ('https://conda.anaconda.org/org/noarch/', ('org', 1))])
```

Anaconda client is set up for `http://localhost:4990/api`.  conda no longer respects this url, it is fetching from `https://conda.anaconda.org/org/osx-64/`

issue_title: improve fault tolerance when channel connection fails
issue_body: Right now conda fails hard on the first channel that it can't connect to.  So an inaccessible or down repository will prevent conda from working at all.  This seems undesirable.  I think a warning should be issued and conda should continue with the next channel.  Some option like `--strict` could be used to indicate that any channel failure aborts conda.  But in the general case the current behavior can make conda hard to use when remote systems out of the user's control are not accessible from the local system.

issue_title: What does package name ending with '@' mean?
issue_body:
I see several places where packages ending with '@' are being referred to, in conda code:

conda/models/dist.py
```python
 72     def is_feature_package(self):
 73         return self.dist_name.endswith('@')
```

conda/cli/main_search.py
```python
185         if '@' in name:
186             continue
```

tests/test_resolve.py
```python
221     def test_anaconda_mkl_2(self):
222         # to test "with_features_depends"
223         dists = r.install(['anaconda 1.5.0', 'python 2.7*', 'numpy 1.7*', 'mkl@'])
```

I don't see any of '@' or 'with_features_depends' anywhere in conda docs. Am I looking at the wrong place?

* http://conda.pydata.org/docs/spec.html#package-match-specifications (for '@')
* http://conda.pydata.org/docs/search.html?q=with_features_depends&check_keywords=yes&area=default (for 'with_features_depends')

Can we please have this documented somewhere? Even after going through http://conda.pydata.org/docs/building/bpp.html I couldn't figure out a scenario using conda-build and conda index to create repodata.json with `with_feature_depends`

issue_title: get_bucket_policy() doesn't return correct output in one case
issue_body:
The following code shows a bug in  get_bucket_policy()  after setting two bucket policies with different prefixes. 

```
from minio import Minio
from minio.policy import Policy
from minio.error import ResponseError

client = Minio('s3.amazonaws.com', secure=True, access_key='xxx', secret_key='xxx/xxx/xxx')
bucket_name = 'mybucket'

try:
    client.set_bucket_policy(bucket_name, '', Policy.WRITE_ONLY)
    client.set_bucket_policy(bucket_name, 'public/', Policy.READ_ONLY)
    print(client.get_bucket_policy(bucket_name))
    print(client.get_bucket_policy(bucket_name, 'public/'))
except ResponseError as err:
    print(err)
```

The code above returns the following output:
```
none
readonly
```
which is wrong, it should be:

```
writeonly
readonly
```

issue_body:
I have merged two pull request by @jaroel, which themselves passed fine on Travis, but now on master Travis fails. This is only the [QA job](https://travis-ci.org/plone/plone.api/jobs/279411379).

- `isort` complains. When I try it locally it complains too, about `portal.py` which has the date/datetime commits in the wrong sort order. - In the `docs` jobs we get a too new `Products.ZCatalog` which cannot do `from AccessControl.Permission import getPermissionIdentifier`. AccessControl indeed does not have that. Oh, I see that this part [failed](https://travis-ci.org/plone/plone.api/jobs/279155753) on the original pull requests as well. And the `docs` part of Travis actually passes on master. It is just the isort/flake8 that fails.

Maybe we should just retry later.

issue_title: Testing for Tasking System Bugfix
issue_body:
This is a test for [Issue 2770](https://pulp.plan.io/issues/2770).

When a sync happens the message flow goes from httpd -> broker -> resource_manager -> broker -> worker. There are two tests, each asserting a message being sent to the broker.

The first test tests httpd dispatching a task while the broker is down:

1. Start with a healthy Pulp system with no tasks executing
2. Stop the broker
3. Sync a single repo. The call will fail with a 500 error, not great but expected.
4. Check for tasks in the waiting state, and observe that there should be 0 if this bug is fixed. If this bug is not fixed I expect there to be 1.

The second test tests the resource_manager dispatching a task to the broker. This is for rarely occurring situations because if the resource_manager receives the message from the broker the chances of it being down when it goes to send a message back are rare. The only way I know to test it is by adding a line like the patch below.

```patch
diff --git a/server/pulp/server/async/tasks.py b/server/pulp/server/async/tasks.py
index 0d57197..725be2c 100644
--- a/server/pulp/server/async/tasks.py
+++ b/server/pulp/server/async/tasks.py
@@ -468,6 +468,7 @@ class Task(PulpTask, ReservedTaskMixin):
         group_id = kwargs.pop('group_id', None)
 
         try:
+            raise KeyError('oh no')
             async_result = super(Task, self).apply_async(*args, **kwargs)
         except:
             if 'task_id' in kwargs:
```

1. Start with a healthy Pulp system with no tasks executing
2. Patch the installation as shown above and restart services because the patch is applied
3. sync a repo
4. assert that the task enters the state 'error' soon

issue_title: nquads parser creates one context per triple in the default graph
issue_body:
In nquads, the 4th component (graph id) of each line can be omitted, meaning that the triple belongs to the default graph.

Currently, the nquads parser puts every such triple in a different context, identified by a fresh BNode, which is clearly wrong.

E.g.:
```python
from rdflib import ConjunctiveGraph, Dataset
from rdflib.parser import StringInputSource

ds = ConjunctiveGraph()

src = StringInputSource("""
<http://example.org/s1> <http://example.org/p1> <http://example.org/o1> .
<http://example.org/s2> <http://example.org/p2> <http://example.org/o2> .
<http://example.org/s3> <http://example.org/p3> <http://example.org/o3> <http://example.org/g3> .
""")

ds.parse(src, format="nquads")

assert len(ds) == 3, len(g)
assert len(list(ds.contexts())) == 2, len(list(ds.contexts()))
```

(Brief description of the problem in no more than 2 sentences.)
Ideally, ResNet is about 6 times bigger than AlexNet in terms of compute and with a power GPU like Volta or Pascal, I anticipated that the performance difference between the two models to be about 2-3 times or even more. However, I am getting surprisingly low performance for AlexNet while ruinning the examples/image-classification/train_imagenet.py example on my Tesla V100/TitanXp gpu. I am getting roughly 540 img/sec with a batch size of 64 with synthetic data for both the models. When I experimented it with Gluon model https://github.com/apache/incubator-mxnet/blob/master/example/gluon/image_classification.py I was able to get a difference of about 8times. issue_title: Could somebody tell me how to use softmaxoutput function exactly?
issue_body:
Hi everyone.I have a question about the realization the softmaxoutput of loss layer which confuses me a lot. I wrote a simple CNN network below.just three convolution layers.
###### 

```
data=mx.symbol.Variable('data')
conv1=mx.symbol.Convolution(data=data,kernel=(9,9),pad=(0,0),num_filter=64,name="conv1")
relu1=mx.symbol.Activation(data=conv1,act_type="relu",name="relu1")
conv2=mx.symbol.Convolution(data=relu1,kernel=(1,1),pad=(0,0),num_filter=32,name="conv2")
relu2=mx.symbol.Activation(data=conv2,act_type="relu",name="relu2")
conv3=mx.symbol.Convolution(data=relu2,kernel=(5,5),pad=(0,0),num_filter=1,name="conv3")
softmax=mx.symbol.SoftmaxOutput(data=conv3,name='softmax')
mynet=softmax
```
###### 

 I used the infer_shape() method to get the dimension of label ,here I suppose my size of data is (50,1,33,33)
###### 

arg,out,aux=mynet.infer_shape(data=(50,1,33,33))
I get the result:
 arg
[(50L, 1L, 33L, 33L), (64L, 1L, 9L, 9L), (64L,), (32L, 64L, 1L, 1L), (32L,), (1L, 32L, 5L, 5L), (1L,), (50L, 1L, 21L)]
###### 

Actually I really don't know why the dimension of label becomes (50L,1L,21L) , I am not familar with the fuction of softmax,in my mind ,the softmax is just a combination of exp() and normalization. 

 I refer to the "numpy_softmax.py" in the file of example/numpy-ops .here,I see the code which seems like softmax.However, the function doesn't seem to reduce the dimention of label.
###### 

def forward(self, in_data, out_data):
        x = in_data[0]
        y = out_data[0]
        y[:] = np.exp(x - x.max(axis=1).reshape((x.shape[0], 1)))
        y /= y.sum(axis=1).reshape((x.shape[0], 1))
###### 

So I have two methods.One is that I should know exactly the softmaxout in the mxnet,then use the same method to change my label,and reduce the dimention of label.But I am new to the MXnet,could someone tell me the principle of softmaxout fuction,why the dimension of label reduces. Another method I want to ask is whether I  need to use the softmaxoutput  ,can I  make the conv3 data to be my output,and how? I hope some experts can give me some advice , thank you so much.

issue_title: Books.ipynb (and maybe the rest of the textbook) uses out-of-date methods
issue_body:
* Data access: this one, we had already seen during the class: Project Gutenberg now requires a click to access the data, so the automatic remote url load fails.  Users can work around it by manually downloading the files and uploading them back to data8, but that sort of defeats the purpose.

A good alternative suggested by Saul would be to have cached copies of key datasets in a data8 url, and update  the examples to pull from here instead.  This would still show folks the use of the http-based data access (better than storing them as local files) while circumventing remote blocks.  You could add a comment along the lines of:

```
# We will access this remote data
#huck_finn_url = 'http://www.gutenberg.org/cache/epub/76/pg76.txt'
# from a Berkeley cache so the whole class can fetch the data.
huck_finn_url = 'http://berkeley-cache.data8.org/gutenberg/epub/76/pg76.txt'# When working from home with a local copy of the tools, you can try using
# the original address.
```

or similar... There are other solutions (full-on proxy server for all data requests, etc), just wanted to mention the issue as a current blocker so you can consider the options.


* There is a missing "import numpy as np" that should be added to the first cell or at least before numpy is used.

issue_title: Run ASGI application constructors in a sync thread
issue_body: As they may be blocking.

issue_title: DockerClient.secrets is not a property
issue_body: `DockerClient.secrets` is not decorated with `property` and so must be called rather than just accessed. This is inconsistent with the docs and similar collections on the client attribute (e.g. images, containers, etc.).

issue_title: Cannot pull from private registry with HTTP authentication
issue_body:
We have a private (v1) registry accessible via HTTPS which is protected with HTTP authentication (credentials set up via `.dockercfg`). Unfortunately, using docker-py to pull from this registry via
``` .py
import docker.client
client = docker.client.Client()
client.pull('our.registry.server:1234/bla', tag='latest')
```
doesn't work:
```
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "<stdin>", line 1, in <module>
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/docker/client.py", line 747, in pull
    repository, insecure=insecure_registry
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/docker/auth/auth.py", line 59, in resolve_repository_name
    return expand_registry_url(parts[0], insecure), parts[1]
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/docker/auth/auth.py", line 38, in expand_registry_url
    "HTTPS endpoint unresponsive and insecure mode isn't enabled."
docker.errors.DockerException: HTTPS endpoint unresponsive and insecure mode isn't enabled.
```
The problem is that in `auth.py`, `expand_registry_url` determins whether `https://our.registry.server:1234` works by accessing that URL (with something added) and aborting if any HTTP status code >= 400 is returned. Since we use HTTP authentication, this yields the HTTP status code 401, which causes the function to raise the above exception.

While this problem can be overcome in `expand_registry_url` by adding `https://`, its caller `resolve_repository_name` explicitly checks for that and bails out if a scheme is specified.

issue_title: When GenerateAssemblyInfo Is False, Project File (.csproj) and Project Properties Are No Longer in Sync
issue_body:
# Issue Title

If you add

`<GenerateAssemblyInfo>false</GenerateAssemblyInfo>`

to a .NET Core project file (.csproj), entering information in the project's properties package tab no longer updates the project file and any package information already within the project file no longer is displayed within the project's properties package tab.

# General

.NET Core Version 2.0

Save the following markup as a Visual Studio project file (.csproj).

`<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">

  <PropertyGroup>
    <TargetFramework>netcoreapp2.0</TargetFramework>
    <Version>1.1.0.0</Version>
    <Authors>Author Test</Authors>
    <Company>Test Company</Company>
    <Product>AssemblyTest DLL</Product>
    <Description>A test of the description field</Description>
    <Copyright>Copyright 2018</Copyright>
    <PackageTags>tag1, tag2</PackageTags>
    <AssemblyVersion>1.2.0.0</AssemblyVersion>
    <FileVersion>1.3.0.0</FileVersion>
    <GenerateAssemblyInfo>false</GenerateAssemblyInfo>
  </PropertyGroup>

</Project>`

Open this project file within Visual Studio 2017.  Edit the project's properties either in the package tab GUI or edit the properties through project file directly.  Updating one will no longer update the other.  Remove the "GenerateAssemblyInfo" tag/property from the project file and the information will be in sync again.

Is this by design?

issue_title: Referencing a .NET Standard class library from a .NET Core Web App fails during runtime
issue_body:
# Summary
Referencing a .NET Standard 1.4 or 1.6 class library from a .NET Core 1.1 Web App or a .NET Standard 2.0 class library from a .NET Core 2.0 Web App fails at runtime. 

For the .NET Core 1.1 Web App and .NET Standard 1.4 and 1.6 class libraries I've used Visual Studio 2017 Enterprise version 15.2 (26430.15). For the .NET Core 2.0 Web App and the .NET Standard 2.0 class library I've used Visual Studio Enterprise 2017 version 15.3.0 preview 6.0.

I'm running on Windows 8.1 Enterprise.

Referencing a .NET Standard 1.0 class library from a .NET Core 1.0 Web App works perfectly fine. 

# Steps to reproduce
In both cases I have created a class library targeting .NET Standard. The class library contains one static class with a static method returning a string. 

```cs
using System;

namespace ClassLibrary
{
    public static class TextProvider
    {
        public static string GetGreeting()
        {
            return "Hello from class library!";
        }
    }
}
```

The web app is created using Visual Studios built-in template for a web project. In the "About" action in the HomeController the static method from the class library is used to get the string and displays it in the view. 

```cs
using System.Diagnostics;
using ClassLibrary;
using Microsoft.AspNetCore.Mvc;
using WebProject.Models;

namespace WebProject.Controllers
{
    public class HomeController : Controller
    {
        public IActionResult Index()
        {
            return View();
        }

        public IActionResult About()
        {
            ViewData["Message"] = TextProvider.GetGreeting();

            return View();
        }

        public IActionResult Contact()
        {
            ViewData["Message"] = "Your contact page.";

            return View();
        }

        public IActionResult Error()
        {
            return View(new ErrorViewModel { RequestId = Activity.Current?.Id ?? HttpContext.TraceIdentifier });
        }
    }
}
```

issue_title: Runtime library loading
issue_body:
# Issue Title

How to load libraries at runtime and resolve their dependencies or have workarounds for that.

# General

I'm currently converting a netfx 4.7 with asp.net core 2.0 application to .net core 2.0/2.1.
The solution looks more or less like this:
1. Web project (has references to the shared projects and business logic projects)
2. Business Logic projects
3. Shared projects (e.g. framework libraries)
4. Test projects
5. Tools

The normal development process looks like this:
1. Build solution in VS 2017 (all projects build to the web project output directory)
2. Run a custom tool on command line (which is part of the solution) to prepare shared test database, this tool loads all libraries (Assembly.Load) with a specific name to prepare the database (e.g. seed data etc.)
3. Develop
4. Run Tests (they use the databases)

The build process on the build machine does a dotnet publish for the web project and this is gets then deployed.

The above process works fine on netfx 4.7 because the build process in VS 2017 copies all libraries and their dependencies from nuget packages to the build directory and i can run the custom tool and build my databases.

On .net core 2.0 the nuget dependencies do not get copied to the build directory during VS 2017 build, so while I see binaries for my test libraries in the build directory several dependencies (e.g. xunit.core.dll) are missing and my tool can't Assembly.Load the test libraries because of that.

As far as I understood e.g. @natemcmaster in several issues related to DependencyContext, that it is possible to properly resolve the nuget dependencies myself but it's a lot of work and probably a lot of hurt too. I got it working fairly nicely, but the whole dependency resolving is kinda poorly documented and I'd rather not use that code.

So I'm looking for alternatives:
The most promising alternative I found is to conditionally include all projects (test/tools etc.) in my web project behind a msbuild property which is only true in DEV, just like in https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/nuget/consume-packages/package-references-in-project-files#adding-a-packagereference-condition

Are there any msbuild properties which are only true during build in Visual Studio?
This would make the above approach easy and msbuild would copy all nuget dependencies because the dependency graph is well known during build as all projects are referenced.

Are there any plans for e.g. 2.1 (couldn't find any) to simplify external library loading during runtime or some MsBuild setting which copies all the nuget dependencies to the output directory just as dotnet publish does?

Any better ideas besides conditionally include all projects, which is kinda ugly (because you need to know that some projects are used conditionally)?

I have a C++ that takes an object of the abstract `DomainBase` class,
```c++
void
generate(const std::shared_ptr<foo::DomainBase> & domain)
```
Since it's using a `shared_ptr` here, I specified that in the pybind11 wrapper code for `DomainBase` like
```c++
py::class_<DomainBase, std::shared_ptr<DomainBase>>(m, "DomainBase");
```

I can now call `generate` for all classes derived in C++ from `DomainBase`, e.g.,
```python
import foo
b = foo.Ball([0.0, 0.0, 0.0], 1.0)
foo.generate(b)
```

Now, I would like to extend this by deriving a class _within Python_, e.g.,
```python
class Hyperboloid(foo.DomainBase):
    def __init__(self):

    def eval(self, x):
         if self.z0 < x[2] and x[2] < self.z1:
            r2 = x[0]**2 + x[1]**2

foo.generate(h)
```
This fails with the

Any hints?

issue_title: Uses of `size_t` and `ptrdiff_t` without including `cstddef`
issue_body:
Many compilers define `size_t` and `ptrdiff_t` but according to the standard these are only guaranteed to be defined in the standard namespace in the `<cstddef>` header.

We came across this issue in xtensor with some flavors of clang.

So the method is to replace all uses of `size_t` and `ptrdiff_t` with std::size_t and `std::ptrdiff_t` and include `cstddef`.

issue_title: Imports are done incorrectly in Stripe Python library
issue_body:
The Python API package does imports incorrectly. This is what you do:

`from stripe.resource import ...
`

This is what you should do ...

`from resource import ...
`

Why does it matter? Right now, stripe must be at the root of the web server. There is no reason for this and many web sites have packages such as Stripe in a subdirectory. Changing the imports would enable this without any disadvantages.

Support noted that there is an expectation that Stripe is installed globally. That is impossible on Google App Engine and is also not a best practice for people who only have one web server. It makes the server code more fragile because installs must happen before it is moved. Aside from that, fixing this problem would not cause any problems for globally-installed copies of Stripe.

Interestingly, there are comments in your __init__.py which indicate that your developers even know about this bug and haven't fixed it yet.

issue_title: saving BankAccount object after failed verify call results in InvalidRequestError
issue_body:
Attempting to save a BankAccount object after attempting validation results in `InvalidRequestError`.


```
In [20]: account.verify(amounts=[1,1])

...InvalidRequestError: You have tried to verify this bank account 21 times. To continue trying to verify the account, please reach out to us directly.

In [21]: account.save()

InvalidRequestError: A parameter provided in the URL (customer) was repeated as a GET or POST parameter. You can only provide this information as a portion of the URL.
```

Seems to be related to this old bug and fix in Ruby:

https://github.com/stripe/stripe-ruby/issues/52
https://github.com/stripe/stripe-ruby/commit/6a57264758edcec12fa23e9a290e9fec3d2d50eb

I'm able to get around it by using the same workaround listed in linked ruby issue: Namely `thing.refresh()` before `thing.save()`

I'm guessing the `verify` call adds an attribute to the object which is not handled by the `if` chain in `serialize`

issue_title: How is this different from Requests module already available?
During deployment, new instances raise PartitionsOwned error.
issue_body:
We utilize a balanced consumer with rdkafka, running inside a docker container orchestrated by AWS ECS. We run two consumer instances in a group consuming a topic of 2 partitions. I have noticed a trend during deployment, in which the new instances raise PartitionsOwned error. The deployment process involves spinning up new container instances, and deprecating old containers, and switching over connections. This leads me to believe that, for a moment, kafka perceives that 4 consumers are trying to consume 2 partitions, and as kafka law dictates, those newest consumers will receive no partitions. My question is, does pykafka provide an interface to explicitly deregister a consumer. I am hoping to de-register consumer upon a sigint, as provided by container stop. 

Below is how we instantiate consumer, roughly...

```
ConsumerClass(object):
	def __init__(self, consumer_group, auto_commit_enable=True, auto_commit_interval=1000, managed=False, use_rdkafka=False):
	    self.consumer = self.topic.get_balanced_consumer(
	        consumer_group=consumer_group.encode("utf-8"),
	        auto_commit_enable=auto_commit_enable,
	        auto_commit_interval_ms=auth_commit_interval,
	        managed=managed,
	        use_rdkafka=use_rdkafka)
```
We use rdkafka==0.9.1,
Python==3.6
Pykafka==2.5.0

Also, fyi we utilize python tornado in this service, but that doesn't really affect pykafka as the consumer runs on its own thread(s).

How would you suggest de-registering a consumer, is the library handling this ? I did not see in library code where this happens...

Addressing this will make our codebase more maintainable and improve [Better Code Hub](https://bettercodehub.com)'s **Write Code Once** guideline rating! 👍 

Here's the gist of this guideline:
- **Definition** 📖 
Do not copy code.
- **Why**❓ 
When code is copied, bugs need to be fixed in multiple places. This is both inefficient and a source of regression bugs.
- **How** 🔧 
Avoid duplication by never copy/pasting blocks of code and reduce duplication by extracting shared code, either to a new unit or introduce a superclass if the language permits.

You can find more info about this guideline in [Building Maintainable Software](http://shop.oreilly.com/product/0636920049159.do). 📖
