issue_title: SendGrid: duplicate Message-IDs with merge_data/batch send
issue_body:
When doing a batch send with `merge_data`, Anymail's generated Message-ID gets used for all of the individual messages sent to each recipient. Since Message-ID is meant to be unique, this could cause (obscure) problems for some recipients.

Options:
* Force disable SENDGRID_GENERATE_MESSAGE_ID when using merge_data. (Downside: not having id for the message complicates event tracking, which is why Anymail generates them in the first place.)
* Generate a unique Message-ID for each recipient when using merge_data. Would only work if SG allows merge fields in arbitrary headers.
* Rethink the whole [generated Message-ID](https://anymail.readthedocs.io/en/v0.3/esps/sendgrid/#sendgrid-message-id) approach (get rid of it, use a field other than Message-ID, or?)

issue_title: Split up getting started into multiple pages?
issue_body:
As discussed with @astrofrog in https://github.com/astropy/regions/pull/87#issuecomment-264001695, the http://astropy-regions.readthedocs.io/en/latest/getting_started.html page is getting too long and we should probably split it up.

It's not quite clear how to best do it. At the moment there's ~ 16 sections.
Some are very short, is putting each on a sub-page a good solution?
Or maybe just move some of the longer ones (like masks and plotting) on sub-sections?

One point to keep in mind is that we want this to become `astropy.regions`, so the docs should be as similar as possible to the existing structure of Astropy core sub-package docs:
http://astropy.readthedocs.io/en/latest/

I'm pretty sure we do need docs sub-pages, because we'll also add things like MOC (healpix-based regions) here (see #62), so `astropy.regions` will grow in the coming months.

I'm putting this under the 0.2 milestone, and can spend some time on it, but not much.
So comments on what we should do or even help welcome!

issue_title: installation guide update for protobuf dependencies
issue_body:
Without the protobuf binary installation, trying to export some onnx models will fail.

[ONNX](https://github.com/onnx/onnx) recommends:

```
sudo apt-get install protobuf-compiler libprotoc-dev
pip install onnx
```
Or using conda:
```
conda install -c conda-forge onnx
```

issue_title: References to Swagger should say OpenAPI
issue_body:
[Starting January 1st 2016 the Swagger Specification has been donated to the Open API Initiative (OAI) and is the foundation of the OpenAPI Specification.](http://swagger.io/)

All references to Swagger in our docs should reflect its current name (or both, eg "OpenAPI (fka Swagger)") and link the [OpenAPI-Specification](https://github.com/OAI/OpenAPI-Specification).

issue_title: Must have VPN to connect my web
Implement TLS-SNI-02 challenge support
issue_body:
Right now Certbot supports TLS-SNI-01. The latest ACME spec specifies TLS-SNI-02. We can implement support for the newer challenge type separately from implement the whole of the latest ACME spec.

Pebble (https://github.com/letsencrypt/pebble) implements TLS-SNI-02.

issue_title: Authenticator and installer not parsing arguments the same way
issue_body:
Hi folks,

I am trying to write an installer plugin. I need an optional argument (parameter) from the user that has one or more values (i.e. `nargs='+'`). In the config namespace passed to my plugin's `__init__`, I can see the parameter I specify, and it works as expected:

- [x] When not specified, the config value is `None`
- [x] When specified with no values, I get an error "expected at least one argument"
- [x] When specified with a single value, the config is `["value0"]`
- [x] When specified with e.g. 2 values, the config is `["value0","value1"]`

These are all the correct behavior that I would expect from argparse.

However, if I combine this with the `--dns-route53` authenticator plugin, I run into some problems. The config my plugin get when certbot starts up is still correct in all cases, the `dns-route53` authenticator runs successfully, but then in the last case above (multiple values), I am getting `certbot: error: unrecognized arguments: value1` (along with a usage printout) before my plugin is run, and then certbot quits. 

If the `dns-route53` authenticator is skipped (i.e. because an existing certificate is found), then my installer plugin is executed correctly.

Suggestions? I'm not really set up for other authentication methods so I haven't been able to try those.

macOS 10.13.5
Python 3.6.5
certbot==0.25.1
certbot-dns-route53==0.25.1

issue_title: Ambiguity in your documentation
issue_body:
https://github.com/edoburu/django-parler/blob/master/docs/configuration.rst  

The section `PARLER_ENABLE_CACHING` (Line 110 I believe) states:  

> If needed, caching can be disabled. This is likely not needed.  

I find the latter sentence confusing. Are you stating that the configuration line `PARLER_ENABLE_CACHING = True` is probably not needed, or that it is likely that one doesn't want to disable caching?  

You might argue that my English comprehension is lacking, which I will not disagree. However, in my opinion documentation should be as unambiguous as possible and assuming that most people using your django app are non-native English speakers, you might want to communicate this point differently.

issue_title: Feature: support language variant as django translation
issue_body:
A language variant should be considered to be supported, even if it's not defined in the PARLER_LANGUAGES (just as what django does). 

For example,

In settings.py
```
PARLER_LANGUAGES = {
    None: (
        {'code': 'en',},
        {'code': 'fr',},
    ),
    'default': {
        'fallbacks': ['en'],          
        'hide_untranslated': False,   
    }
}
```
Then 
```
assert model.safe_translation_getter('title', language_code='fr') == 'bonjour'
assert model.safe_translation_getter('title', language_code='fr-ca') == 'bonjour'
assert model.safe_translation_getter('title', language_code='en') == 'hello'
```
Or 
```
with switch_language(model, 'fr-ca'):
    assert model.title == 'bonjour'
```

issue_title: Urllib3HttpConnection should have separate client_cert and client_key arguments
issue_body:
Many deployments have the client cert and key in separate files, for security purposes.  You usually want much tighter control of who can read the key file.

Note that the function that is eventually called with these arguments is `ssl.wrap_socket()` https://docs.python.org/3.5/library/ssl.html#ssl.wrap_socket which has separate keyfile and certfile arguments (although certfile supports a file with both the cert and key in it).

it builds the most recently released version of the notebook which at this time is `notebook-4.0.6`.

This seemed like an undesirable asymmetry for what should be equivalent commands, but it may be that there's something about `pip3` that I don't know. Otherwise, I figure it's a bug, and I don't know enough about `docutils` and the differences between `pip` and `pip3` to fix it myself.

issue_title: Print events from matplotlib event handlers get swallowed
issue_body:
Consider the following code in the notebook:
```python
%pylab notebook

fig, ax = plt.subplots()
ax.plot(np.random.rand(10))

def onclick(event):
    print ('button=%d, x=%d, y=%d, xdata=%f, ydata=%f'%(
        event.button, event.x, event.y, event.xdata, event.ydata))

cid = fig.canvas.mpl_connect('button_press_event', onclick)
```

these events never print anywhere.

The code does run, as can be seen by this code from @tacaswell: 

```python
%pylab notebook

fig, ax = plt.subplots()
ax.plot(np.random.rand(10))
collector = []
def onclick(event):
    collector.append('button=%d, x=%d, y=%d, xdata=%f, ydata=%f'%(
        event.button, event.x, event.y, event.xdata, event.ydata))

cid = fig.canvas.mpl_connect('button_press_event', onclick)
```

After clicking a couple of times:

```python
In [2]: collector
['button=1, x=201, y=275, xdata=2.196853, ydata=0.615071',
 'button=1, x=305, y=360, xdata=4.100549, ydata=0.770019',
 'button=1, x=421, y=223, xdata=6.205388, ydata=0.520279']
```

Somehow we're swallowing that stdout completely, I'm not 100% sure why right now. But to the user, it feels very puzzling. Given the equivalent code works just fine at the terminal, we should try to make it work without jumping through additional hoops in the notebook.

issue_title: Create a troubleshooting guide
issue_body:
Largely speaking, the most consistent problem we run into is with kernels spawning (we have a lot of related issues for this). While we double down on fixing the core issues, we should also have a semi-handy guide (`TROUBLESHOOTING.md`) to help us help others.

Here's a starter:

## ERROR_EXECUTING Kernel not connected

* Open up the dev tools
* Report any errors seen
* Install the python kernel with `python -m ipykernel install --user`
* Fully restart nteract

issue_title: Simpler request helper functions that accept ``bytes`` and ``unicode`` args
issue_body:
This is related to a bug I wrote a while back #108 for Klein and [8905](https://twistedmatrix.com/trac/ticket/8905) for Twisted. It would make writing code more pleasant if certain functions of the ``Request`` object would accept both ``bytes`` and ``unicode``. This would also bridge gaps between writing Py2 and Py3 code. This issue was primarily created because there are inconsistencies with:

* [Headers](https://twistedmatrix.com/documents/current/api/twisted.web.http_headers.Headers.html) (param can be ``bytes`` or ``unicode``)
* [adding cookies](https://twistedmatrix.com/documents/current/api/twisted.web.http.Request.html#addCookie) (param can be ``bytes`` or ``unicode``)
* getting cookies (param can only be ``bytes``)
* getting ``request.args`` (param can only be ``bytes``)

It is easy to do something like ``'unicode or bytes key'.encode('utf-8')`` before passing it to the various getter/setter functions, however the more popular Python web frameworks never force users to do this and Klein should follow suite. I'm well aware that these are upstream Twisted issues, but it's extremely noticeable in Klein. My hope is to deploy a solution here, test it out with the people who use Klein, then merge them upstream.

issue_title: A REPL-style API
issue_body:
Hi there,

This is a follow-up to a discussion that I recently had with @wintersteiger, and I think I might have mentioned it in passing this to @NikolajBjorner last October, too.

Multiple of the provers I've worked on (Dafny and F*) use Z3's pipe-based interface (`-in`) rather than using Z3's API directly: they start an instance of Z3, send their commands in SMT2 format, and wait for a response.  Z3 has no good way to tell the consumer that it's done with a particular command, so in F* batches of commands are terminated with `(echo "Done!")`, and F* continuously scans Z3's output for that marker.  

It would be very nice to have a clean API to do this sort of things.  The best I've found online is a 2016 comment by @NikolajBjorner saying it was a [somewhat recurrent question](https://stackoverflow.com/questions/38527534/z3opt-c-api-parse-smtlib2-formula-from-file) :) There's `Z3_parse_smtlib2_string` et. al, but it looks like the amount of effort needed to match `-in` with that would be high.

The API I'm proposing looks like this: clients would start by initializing a fresh prover (in one of the supported `-in` modes, in my case SMT2); this would give them a handle `p` on that prover.  Then, they would make repeated calls to `std::string p.eval(std::string input)`.  `p` would preserve its state across invocations, meaning that consecutive calls would behave just like adding more text on stdin. A final call on `p` would clear its internal memory.

(It would be even better if `p.eval` had a way to indicate to the caller that the input was incomplete, of course).

There's a motivation for this beyond aesthetics and immediate convenience: I'm transpiling F* to Javascript and running it against a version of Z3 compiled to WebAssembly.   In that setting, Z3 runs on the same thread as F*, and reading from stdin just calls a function.  I cannot implement the traditional style where system pipes serve as the synchronization mechanism between Z3's and its client: if I call Z3's `main()`, it will read whatever is available on stdin, then get EOF, and exit (thus losing its state and preventing incremental solving).

I looked around with @wintersteiger's help (thanks!) to try to see what the implementation of the API suggested above might look like, and put together a tiny proof-of-concept: https://github.com/cpitclaudel/z3/commit/50a505673111cc3991244c894ff58130d6a26d48#diff-49d45889919896b3e21acf88e1a47826R3087 .  What I'm doing there is generalizing the smt2parser to make its `operator()` method take a scanner and read from that.  It's not the right way to do things (it doesn't handle errors, it introduces that m_scanner pointer, etc.), but hopefully it helps clarify things.

Let me know if I can add details or clarify further.

Thanks!

issue_title: Why `-fno-strict-aliasing` applied to all UNIX like systems apart from Darwin?

See https://github.com/Z3Prover/z3/blob/69aa5ca877f5de0a2c00515d0fe86a797b95701a/scripts/mk_util.py#L2445 .

* Why is the `-fno-strict-aliasing` flag being used?

issue_title: Use singletons or factories for all time zone classes
issue_labels: [{'id': 149492527, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/dateutil/dateutil/labels/enhancement', 'name': 'enhancement', 'color': '84b6eb', 'default': True}, {'id': 149492528, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/dateutil/dateutil/labels/help%20wanted', 'name': 'help wanted', 'color': '159818', 'default': True}, {'id': 667039516, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/dateutil/dateutil/labels/low-difficulty', 'name': 'low-difficulty', 'color': '0e8a16', 'default': False}, {'id': 667039615, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/dateutil/dateutil/labels/medium-difficulty', 'name': 'medium-difficulty', 'color': 'fbca04', 'default': False}, {'id': 177050120, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/dateutil/dateutil/labels/time%20zones', 'name': 'time zones', 'color': 'c7def8', 'default': False}]
issue_body:
Similar issue to #342, which seeks to add caches to time zone functions to make lookups faster, we should *also* try to always return the exact same object, because Python's model is that two `datetime` objects are in the same timezone if and only if `dt1.tzinfo is dt2.tzinfo`. I have two blog posts on why this matters for datetime semantics:

1. [A curious case of non-transitive datetime comparison](https://blog.ganssle.io/articles/2018/02/a-curious-case-datetimes.html)
2. [Semantics of timezone-aware datetime arithmetic](https://blog.ganssle.io/articles/2018/02/aware-datetime-arithmetic.html)

As such, as a general rule we should try and return the same object for the same inputs. The highest priority objects to turn into factories:

- [ ] [`tzlocal`](https://dateutil.readthedocs.io/en/latest/tz.html#dateutil.tz.tzlocal) - See note below
- [ ] [`tzrange`](https://dateutil.readthedocs.io/en/latest/tz.html#dateutil.tz.tzrange)
- [ ] `tzwin`
- [ ] [`tzical`](https://dateutil.readthedocs.io/en/latest/tz.html#dateutil.tz.tzical)

## Note on `tzlocal` and `tzwinlocal`
Both `tzlocal` and `tzwinlocal` bind the time zone information *when constructed*, so if on a linix machine you do:

```python
from dateutil import tz

# Time zone is America/New_York
LOCAL1 = tz.tzlocal()
LOCAL2 = tz.tzlocal()

# change_timezone_to is an imaginary function that changes your machine zone
change_timezone_to('America/Pacific')
LOCAL3 = tz.tzlocal()
```

`LOCAL1` and `LOCAL2` will refer to something like `EST5EDT` and `LOCAL3` will refer to something like `PST8PDT`. This is complicated by the fact that the way `tzlocal` is *currently* implemented, this information is only *partially* bound at compile time, which is a bug.

It is not clear what the correct behavior is when a local time zone changes during the course of a program run. For now, the best way to implement a "tzlocal factory" that preserves the old behavior is to check whether the cached `tzlocal` would be the same as the one that you *would* construct and return a new object if and only if they would not be the same.

Ideally this would be done with minimal overhead, because part of the reason for using singletons is that they are much faster to "construct" than a new object every time.

issue_title: Autoconfigured web3
issue_body:
### What was wrong?

In code examples, and short scripts, it's a real PITA to show the ramp-up needed to get web3 working. It conveys that things are going to be tedious (an unfair first impression, I hope):

```
from web3 import Web3, IPCProvider

w3 = Web3(IPCProvider())
```

### How can it be fixed?

Instead, I would love to have an autoconfigured web3, especially for some default cases, like localhost on 8545, and the geth IPC file.

```
from web3.auto import w3
```

This could try to connect to IPC in common locations and then localhost:8545

Or if you are running both IPC and HTTP, and want to give it a hint, that might look like:

```
from web3.auto.http import w3
```

A possible solution is to use one ``cat ref[i].fa >> node.fa`` command for each fasta file.

issue_title: Loopback does not work with asyncio
issue_body: Loopback interface always raises `writeTimeoutError` on any write using asyncio SerialTransport. It happens because asyncio implementation sets timeout to 0 (`aio.py:L54`) and loopback checks if it can write a data in given timeout (obviously it can't).

issue_title: Population section path distance inconsistency
issue_body:
The section path distance as calculated from a population seems inconsistent with that of individual neurons. This can be tested with a small sample of synthesized morphologies, where the soma positions of each cell differ:

```
import numpy as np
import neurom as nm
from neurom.io.utils import get_morph_files

DIR = 'path/to/some/morphologies'

pop = nm.load_neurons(DIR)
nrns = [nm.load_neuron(f) for f in get_morph_files(DIR)]

rad_dist_pop = nm.get('section_radial_distances', pop)

rad_dist_nrns = []
for nrn in nrns:
    rad_dist_nrns.extend( nm.get('section_radial_distances', nrn))

rad_dist_nrns = np.array(rad_dist_nrns)

print np.alltrue(rad_dist_pop == rad_dist_nrns)
```

Expected output: `True`, observed output: `False`.

order.type是枚举变量，这样会导致这一判断一直是False，造成虽然指定了LimitOrder的价格却还是按照close price成交。

这样修改后

```python
from rqalpha.model.order import ORDER_TYPE
...
        if order.type == ORDER_TYPE.LIMIT:
            deal_price = order.frozen_price
```

问题消失。

issue_title: Multiple threads being used by asyncio ApplicationRunner?!?!?!
issue_body:
Hi guys, I'm working with some Python 3 components that use asyncio and are run as guest workers in my Crossbar controller.

I've been having some weird issues lately that *seem* as if they could be threading-related deadlocks. Initially I was like, _that can't be happening, asyncio is single-threaded right?_ but when I ran `htop` and filtered the view to show one of my components it seems to tell a different story.

Here is a screenshot with `uvloop` powering things:
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/248980/27173170-fead6452-51b7-11e7-8ae5-0d71d5102e02.png)

and here is another with the stock `asyncio` loop:
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/248980/27173197-1c6e8502-51b8-11e7-8d34-b94268840a28.png)

and another with the stock `asyncio` loop after hitting a RPC end-point hosted by this component:
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/248980/27173243-498e0594-51b8-11e7-8b7b-5087ea2298d2.png)

as you can see, `htop` indicates that threads are in use. There are differences between `uvloop` (which seems to use a fixed pool of 4 threads) and the stock loop (which starts with 1 thread and then scales up when it handles an RPC call) but overall the story is the same: threads are in use.

Is this normal? Is there something wrong with my components? Could this cause deadlock issues with my component (which is using a aiomysql connection pool attacked via a decorator to the RPC function being called)?

When Active Timestamp or Pending Timestamp changes a parent should send unicast Data Response a SED. The Data Response message should contain Network Data (if changed), Active Timestamp and (optionally) Pending Timestamp. Use case: we want to distribute command-line tools written in Python to Linux users, and tell users "it'll work as long as you have Python 3.4, 3.5 or 3.6".

issue_title: Enable directory notifications in watchman
issue_body:
Invalidating the directory listing for a particular directory currently does not recursively invalidate to the root of the directory tree. The easiest way to explain the reason for this is that:

* listing one directory does not depend on listing another
* but execution of a particular PathGlobs object will consume directory listings independently in a certain order

In order to properly invalidate, we need to invalidate even empty directories. For example, removing a tree of empty directories should fire an event for each of them.

issue_title: Proper implementation of `**` globs in the v2 engine.
issue_body:
AFAICT, we don't have a proper implementation of trailing `**` matching in the new engine. This needs to be repaired, but is non-trivial.

Expectations:

  `**` -> matches everything, recursively.
  `dir/**` -> matches everything under `dir`, recursively.
  `**/*.py` -> matches every dir recursively, searching for files with a `.py` extension.

It may also make sense to define a formal spec for our globs implementation so we can code to the spec.

References:

- A spec that we are close to matching: https://git-scm.com/docs/gitignore
- A non-spec: https://github.com/bazelbuild/bazel/blob/master/src/test/java/com/google/devtools/build/lib/vfs/RecursiveGlobTest.java#L58-L76

issue_title: If the same Optionable is installed in multiple places, its deprecated options scope can collide
issue_body:
See title.

I encountered this internally where we were manually installing the `jvm` checkstyle tasks at the end of the `test` goal, while upstream they are not installed in the `lint` goal.

A workaround is to install the task in exactly one location, but that's not ideal. The issue seems to stem from the "default" scope of a task coming from where it is installed (which avoids collisions), but the deprecated scope coming from its definition (which collides).

issue_title: Pants cannot consume 3rdparty python requirements if they depend on pantsbuild.pants
issue_body:
So, this is obviously a limited case. And the details here are:

A) old - the repro was when 1.0.0 landed so details have changed
B) hard to remember - see A

Fsq.io publishes buildgen as a sdist. The Python case was explicitly done to add buildgen support to upstream Pants.

But the Pants bootstrap process will happily pull in the transitive dependencies of all 3rdparty deps. This includes if the dep is a contrib plugin that depends on Pants. That causes Pants to install the depended-upon Pants version into the virtualenv, and therefore have conflicting sources.

A repro can be to try and add https://pypi.python.org/pypi/fsqio.pants.contrib.buildgen.core/1.1.0 and https://pypi.python.org/pypi/fsqio.pants.contrib.buildgen.python/1.1.0 to the requirements.txt and try to bootstrap.

An even easier repro - just add a conflicting pantsbuild.pants version. Adding the below to a `1.3.0dev0` checkout fails.
```diff --git a/3rdparty/python/requirements.txt b/3rdparty/python/requirements.txt
index 91a9229..4e9bf85 100644
--- a/3rdparty/python/requirements.txt
+++ b/3rdparty/python/requirements.txt
@@ -4,6 +4,7 @@ cffi==1.7.0
 coverage>=3.7,<3.8
 docutils>=0.12,<0.13
 fasteners==0.14.1
+pantsbuild.pants==1.2.0rc3
 futures==3.0.5
 isort==4.2.5
 lmdb==0.89
```

 Changing the required version to `1.3.0dev0` allows it to work. Buildgen has been ready to put up an RB since 1.1.0 (just config entries!). But this issue has blocked that.

http://www.bigospace.com/lives/GB/

I can't seem to find the m3u8 sources, but they also use FlashVars:
`param name="FlashVars" value="sid=1000000&uid=22222&ip=183.129.210.18&port=7778"`

Seems like the parameters are generated from `http://live.bigo.tv/0123456789` where `0123456789` might be the `sid`

issue_title: Icon Request: icon-ipfs
issue_body:
This issue requests an icon to represent the logo of [IPFS](https://ipfs.io/) (the Permanent Web).

A single color image that represents the idea (okay, not really single color: it has gray areas that should not appear in the icon):

![(example)](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ipfs/logo/master/ipfs-logo-1024-black.png)

(Source: the [`logo`](https://github.com/ipfs/logo/) repository of IPFS.)

IPFS is a content-addressable distributed file system. Therefore the following use cases for the requested icon are likely:

* An icon before or after a hyperlink that references a file (or a site) which resides in the Permanent Web.

* On a button to indicate an action related to IPFS.

Examples of such buttons:

[![(Publish in IPFS)](https://img.shields.io/badge/%F0%9F%93%A4_Publish_in_IPFS-%E2%86%90-green.svg?style=social)](https://img.shields.io/badge/%F0%9F%93%A4_Publish_in_IPFS-%E2%86%90-green.svg?style=social)   (but with IPFS logo instead of a generic “upload” icon)

[![(Launch IPFS daemon)](https://img.shields.io/badge/%F0%9F%8E%AC_Launch_IPFS_daemon-%E2%86%90-green.svg?style=social)](https://img.shields.io/badge/%F0%9F%8E%AC_Launch_IPFS_daemon-%E2%86%90-green.svg?style=social)   (but with IPFS logo instead of a generic “launch” icon)

[![(Display the list of IPFS peers)](https://img.shields.io/badge/%F0%9F%93%9C_Display_the_list_of_IPFS%20peers-%E2%86%90-green.svg?style=social)](https://img.shields.io/badge/%F0%9F%93%9C_Display_the_list_of_IPFS%20peers-%E2%86%90-green.svg?style=social)   (but with IPFS logo instead of a generic “scroll” icon)

[![(Get a permanent link)](https://img.shields.io/badge/%F0%9F%94%97_Get_a%20permanent_link-%E2%86%90-green.svg?style=social)](https://img.shields.io/badge/%F0%9F%94%97_Get_a%20permanent_link-%E2%86%90-green.svg?style=social)   (but with IPFS logo instead of a generic “link” icon)

issue_title: After upgrade: Uncaught TypeError: Cannot read property 'library' of undefined
issue_body:
After upgrading my webpack packages to:
```
    "@fortawesome/fontawesome": "^1.2.0-6",
    "@fortawesome/fontawesome-free-brands": "^5.0.13",
    "@fortawesome/fontawesome-free-regular": "^5.1.0-3",
    "@fortawesome/fontawesome-free-solid": "^5.1.0-3",
    "@fortawesome/fontawesome-svg-core": "^1.2.0-11",
    "@fortawesome/vue-fontawesome": "^0.1.0-4",
```

And following your guide about adding icons to a fontawesome library via a special icons.js file:
```
import fontawesome from '@fortawesome/fontawesome'
import brands from '@fortawesome/fontawesome-free-brands'
import { faSpinner } from '@fortawesome/fontawesome-free-solid'

fontawesome.library.add(brands, faSpinner)
```

I import above icons.js in my boot.js which also contains the Vuejs main app.


I get the following error when starting the brower (Chrome): 

> Uncaught TypeError: Cannot read property 'library' of undefined

Am I doing something wrong or is something broken in the newest version?

EDIT: 
Forgot to note that when looking in the chrome debugger the fontawesome is NOT undefined. So maybe a timing issue with the imports?

Kind regards,
Henry

issue_title: Support message delivery retry
speed up number_peaks feature by preventing unnecessary calculations
issue_body:
> I thought about this feature a little bit. I think there is great potential to speed up calculations here if we also make this an apply one.
> Also I have an idea for a speed up: At the moment we compare too many values (x_reduced > np.roll). Instead we only have to compare neighbours. We could save only if the neighbour is greater or smaller (-1 if smaller, 1 if bigger). Then we are searching for subsequences. I feel that this way the calculations can be greatly speed up.

This is from the review of pr #186. There is potential in speeding up this feature by preventing unnecessary or doubled comparisons

issue_title: Fail to skip mailto link
issue_body:
### Subject of the issue
Fail to skip mailto link

### Your environment
* Python version: 3
* coursera-dl version: 0.6.0

### Steps to reproduce
> Download course with mailto: link in supplement notes

* Is the problem happening with the latest version of the script?
> Yes

* What is the course that you are trying to access?
> https://www.coursera.org/learn/professional-emails-english/supplement/RvOn9/get-more-from-the-georgia-tech-language-institute

* What is the precise command line that you are using (don't forget to obfuscate
  your username and password, but leave all other information untouched).
> coursera-dl -n -- professional-emails-english

* What are the precise messages that you get?

===================================
Downloading mailto:eslinfo@pe.gatech.edu -> professional-emails-english/01_get-to-know-basic-email-writing-structures/03_punctuation-and-capitalization/04_get-more-from-the-georgia-tech-language-institute_mailto:eslinfo@pe.gatech.edu

    r.raise_for_status()
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.5/site-packages/requests/models.py", line 844, in raise_for_status
    raise HTTPError(http_error_msg, response=self)
requests.exceptions.HTTPError: 404 Client Error: Not Found for url: https://class.coursera.org/professional-emails-english

Traceback (most recent call last):

Skip it

        if "mailto:" not in url:

while loop stuff

issue_title: Speed up Travis tests by using prebuilt Goose
issue_body:
In Boulder, we switched from `go get ...goose` to fetching a pre-built binary, which saved 30 seconds on our Travis builds. The client code can realize the gains by switching test/boulder-start.sh to use `boulder/test/travis_before_install.sh` for its dependency installs. There's a slight penalty because it installs tools like golint that the client doesn't need, but I think it's a net win.

Also, FWIW, you may want to have boulder-start.sh use `boulder/test/run-docker.sh` when running locally (non-Travis), since that avoids installing as many packages on the developer's machine. Unfortunately the Travis build already runs in a Docker container, and we can't run a nested one, so this isn't an option for the Travis tests.

issue_title: Problem with using mps-youtube as a frontend to an mpv control script.
issue_body:
Hey, I've written an [mpc-like](https://github.com/wildefyr/mpvc) control script for use with mpv. It uses mpv's `--input-unix-socket` to send commands to an mpv instance started with it. I've so far attempted putting `--input-unix-socket` into playerargs but this seems to have no effect at all. 

The other option I have tried is setting player to my script `mpvc` and playerargs to `add` which adds the the arg given by mps-youtube (a really long URL, given by google's API). The issue with this is that mps-youtube or the API fails to send me a URL at all in most videos I have tried it on. This is especially annoying as mpv can take a normal youtube URL and play it just fine.

A solution to either one would be a dream as I could use mps-youtube purely as a frontend to adding tracks, then closing it completely while keeping the mpv instance it starts running.

issue_title: ENH: Slicing-aware reader methods
issue_body:
This is an architectural issue at the intersection of #147, #153, and #158.

### The problem

Currently, pims `SliceableIterable` objects wisely forward unknown method calls to their ancestors, which allows methods to be "inherited" from the reader object that opened the file. However, those methods could take frame numbers as their arguments. For example, the Cine and (unmerged) NorpixSeq classes have methods for reading the timestamp of frame *i*. Being able to retrieve a timestamp without reading the entire image can make a large performance difference in some applications. In a similar fashion, these proprietary formats can store other (as yet unsupported) information such as voltages or event markers.

Therefore, methods like `get_time(frameno)` should be aware that they are being called via a child.

### A solution
`FramesSequence`-derived objects that want to support this feature provide the context `_use_map`, which would be used within the child `SliceableIterable` as follows (oversimplified for clarity):

```
# Call get_time() on the parent object
with self._ancestor.use_map(self.indices):
    self._ancestor.get_time(*args, **kw)
```

The reader class would then implement `get_time` as:

```
def get_time(self, frameno):
   actual_frameno = self._indexmap(frameno)
   # Return time of actual_frameno...
```

`use_map()`, `_indexmap()`, etc. would be implemented in the `FramesSequence` base class. `use_map()` could be thread-safe.

Thoughts? I'm open to more elegant solutions, or to the possibility that this is too much of a niche feature!

issue_title: Track dead/alive proxies with authentication
issue_body:
The built-in HttpProxyMiddleware will correctly set up the authentication parameters for proxies sent up from the rotating proxies plugin. However, by doing so, request.meta['proxy'] field is changed to only contain the raw proxy_url, as the credentials are ripped out. When the response is received, rotating proxies will try to mark the proxy as good or dead, but will silently fail because of the 'proxy not in self.proxies' check, resulting in all proxies staying unmarked forever. This behaviour can be verified by using any proxy with authentication and observing that the logstats keeps logging everything as unchecked while scrapy is crawling.

Easy fix is to update '_handle_result' to identify the proxy in self.proxies corresponding to the input request.meta['proxy'], and use this unabridged proxy in the rest of the call. Will make a PR for this unless you have any objections to this approach

issue_title: use `sync` instead of `fetch` in the plugin interface
issue_body: Our user interface is built on `sync` and `reup`, but our internal plugin interface uses `fetch` and `reup` as job names. Probably better to replace `fetch` with `sync` for consistency.

issue_title: Add `peru list` or `peru module list` command
issue_body:
I've been trying to use Peru to maintain dependencies in projects were I simply can't have all dependencies updated in a single commit.

In other words, I need to be able to step through each Peru module, `reup` it, and make a commit to my project's repo. Without doing this, I've got to either be running `peru reup` constantly at some interval (and hoping no more than one dependency is updated within that window of time), or deal with separating monolithic commits of `peru.yaml` into chunks.

Why? Say I've 10 dependencies. I `reup` them all in one commit, test, and one of them breaks the world. Now I've got to somehow bisect my way through my Peru modules. And instead of being able to even use something like `git bisect`, 

It wasn't apparent to me in the docs, but https://github.com/buildinspace/peru/issues/95#issuecomment-77285631 says `peru reup` takes a module name as an argument. Awesome! That gets me halfway there.

Only problem is... how do I make my schmancy little "reup all the things!" shell script know what `peru reup $foo` commands to run before it makes its `git add peru.yaml && git commit` for a single dependency?

For now, I'm following some leads in the "make a shell script understand YAML" department. Could be worse, but it's honestly a little rough and frustrating. If `peru list` or `peru module list` existed, then boom! Easy... done... for anything and everything that can invoke commands on a command-line, with no need to independently parse the YAML that Peru is already parsing anyways.

There's already (I think?) similar "list the things!" functionality for `peru override`.

issue_title: Lab and Luv color conversion bugs
issue_body:
In Scikit-image 0.10.1 the RGB <-> Lab and RGB <-> Luv color conversions appear to have bugs.

Run this test code:
http://connellybarnes.com/dump/skimage_bug

This converts an sRGB 10x10x3 image of uniform randoms in [0, 1] to Lab/Luv, clamps L to the range [0, 0.01], converts back to sRGB, and verifies that the minimum sRGB value is non-negative.

It should output 'lab2rgb: OK' and 'luv2rgb: OK'.

Instead it outputs two tracebacks showing a bug in each method due to negative sRGB values.

You can also run the same clamp test on an input image of your choice. The correct output is a nearly black output image but scikit-image produces some strange colored artifacts.

You can get correct results from MATLAB, and correct codes from Bruce Lindbloom here:

http://www.brucelindbloom.com/index.html?WorkingSpaceInfo.html

In developing an image processing script scikit-image(0.13.0-2) that grabs images off a camera and detects lines using the hough_transform with hough_line_peaks, an error appeared related to a lack of lines in the image.  If there is no output from _prominent_peaks() on line 62 of hough_transform.py, the following error occurs:

Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "theodoliteAlign.py", line 82, in <module>
    a, angle, dist = hough_line_peaks(h,theta,d)
  File "/usr/lib/python3.6/site-packages/skimage/transform/hough_transform.py", line 66, in hough_line_peaks
    return (h, angles[a], dists[d])

issue_title: Add metrics for Unity Virtual Volume.
issue_body:
Add following metrics for Unity Virtual Volume.
Note that virtual volume has two implementations: block & file.
The path and calculation is different for different kind of virtual volume.
* read_iops
* write_iops
* read_mbps
* write_mbps
* total_latency_ms

Related paths:
* block based vvol:
  * sp.\*.storage.vvol.block.\*.reads
  * sp.\*.storage.vvol.block.\*.writes
  * sp.\*.storage.vvol.block.\*.totalIoTime
  * sp.\*.storage.vvol.block.\*.reads
  * sp.\*.storage.vvol.block.\*.readBlocks
  * sp.\*.storage.vvol.block.\*.writes
  * sp.\*.storage.vvol.block.\*.writeBlocks
* file based vvol:
  * sp.\*.storage.vvol.file.\*.readIoTime
  * sp.\*.storage.vvol.file.\*.reads
  * sp.\*.storage.vvol.file.\*.writeIoTime
  * sp.\*.storage.vvol.file.\*.writes
  * sp.\*.storage.vvol.file.\*.reads
  * sp.\*.storage.vvol.file.\*.readBytes
  * sp.\*.storage.vvol.file.\*.writes
  * sp.\*.storage.vvol.file.\*.writeBytes

Formula:
* Total calls for each SP:
> delta(reads) + delta(writes)
* totalIoTime for each SP
> delta(readIoTime) + delta(writeIoTime)
* Total Latency:
> ((spa totalIoTime) + (spb totalTime)) / ((spa total calls) + (spb total calls))/1000

issue_body:
### Describe the problem
<!-- Describe the problem clearly here. -->
We only use a maximum of 8 partitions. We should automatically set the number of partitions instead of requiring users to set themselves.
 
### Source code / logs
<!-- Include any logs or source code that would be helpful to diagnose the problem. If including tracebacks, please include the full traceback. Large logs and files should be attached. Try to provide a reproducible test case that is the bare minimum necessary to generate the problem. -->
This is the only change we should need
```
import multiprocessing

DEFAULT_NPARTITIONS = multiprocessing.cpu_count()
```

issue_title: Versioning
issue_body:
Sorry to bring this issue up again but it's making my life maintaining the Yocto meta layer difficult.

Releases/tags in my opinion cannot be a moving target. Release v1.11.0 started at commit 74ff66da50210e6b9edc3157411bad95c209740f and has now changed to 6626123bf36ff4e6399081a6972d547ef6498bef so it has 2 parents without even a minor version bump. This has been the case for v1.10.0 and previous releases also and isn't maintainable in my view.

I'm up for having a 1.11.x branch which receives minor bug fixes but periodic releases from that branch should have a minor version bump also.

Interested in your opinions on this?

issue_title: Illegal instruction on Up Squared computer with Pentium processor
issue_body:
| Camera Model                       | {D400 } | 
| Firmware Version                   | 05.08.15.00| 
| Operating System & Version |   Linux Ubuntu 16.04  | 
| Kernel Version (Linux Only)    |  4.13.0                                        | 
| Platform                                 | [Up Squared computer with Pentium N4200 processor](https://up-shop.org/up-boards/97-up-squared-pentium-quad-core-8gb-memory64gb-emmc.html)  |
| SDK Version                            |  v2.10.2 | 

### Issue Description
I tried to compile and run the latest (v2.10.2) librealsense on a Up Squared computer with Pentium N4200 processor.  The compiling was smooth and successful, but I got an error message when I run any librealsense tools or examples:
>  Illegal instruction (core dumped)

Actually I have run into the same issue with my own program on Up Squared computer with Celeron processor before, and I solved that time by add -msse3 flag to compiler (since Celeron does not support AVX2 instructions).

However, this time I still got the error even after I added -msse3 flag... So I was wondering anyone knows how to solve this? or has the same issue?

Thanks

issue_title: REARCH: finish changing high-level cmds (used from CLI) to not print to screen
issue_body: This effort is partially complete and has been discussed in the team.  Being recorded here for completeness and as a reminder to finish.  The issue is that many of the high-level commands were printing results to stdout, instead of passing them back as a return value.  This limited the utility of the high-level interface to just the Unix CLI,  but it's preferable to be able to call the high-level interface from Python code too.

issue_title: cookbook example queries fail
Decentralize Matrix discovery room
issue_body:
## Problem Definition

The matrix discovery room isn't used to actually find peers, but instead to trigger any server whose any user joined it to keep track of every other member of this room. This makes the servers know about all the matrix users in the network, allowing its users to listen for events from these users (e.g. presence changes) and also to use the _search user directory_ feature to search for users matching a given ETH address.

Today, this room isn't listened on, but it's "hardcoded" in the config to be on the `transport01.raiden.network` server (any private deploy can change it with a config option, of course). As these rooms aren't actually listened on, they shouldn't be a spam vector, and therefore it's safe for the users to join a similar room on each available server. That will get rid of this last point of centralization on current transport implementation, as well as open the path for more direct server list keeping, like an on-chain registry.

## Proposal:

The users should join a "discovery room" on each available server, so the server can keep track of it for its users.

Hi,

I would like to release a version 3.1.0.1 based on version 3.1 but with the fix regarding raising a TypeError when index is None (backport the "#5: Ignore None values in UnIndex instead of raising a TypeError." fix).

Indeed for now, for example in last Plone 5.1.x release, it is not possible to use version 3.1 because of this blocking and it is no more possible to use version 3.1.1 because it relies on ZODB instead ZODB3 and requires ZODB 4+ that is not compatible with Plone5...

So in the meantime, I would like to build a new version based on 3.1 release that fixes the TypeError so it is useable on Plone5.  We would like to adapt various packages to be able to handle "not" ZCatalog queries.

Does that seem correct to you?  Or do you see any other way to use version 3+ with Plone5?

Thank you,

Gauthier

issue_title: Create a nicer UI for importing
issue_body:
This is related to pull request #16, which already updates the Import tab in the ZMI.

Perceived problem: the Import tab is too complicated.

Initial idea:
- Move the tarball import to a separate tab. Any checkboxes or other options on the current Import tab have no influence at all on importing a tarball. Tab name: Tarball or Archive.
- The rest of the Import tab can stay. The changes I did to the UI in pull request #16 can probably be reverted for this tab. Maybe remove 'Import all steps' and instead have a 'select/deselect steps' button/checkbox, although that might mean adding some javascript, which is currently totally not in use. Rename the 'Include dependencies?' checkbox to 'Include dependencies of selected steps?', because that is what this checkbox does. It has nothing to do with dependency profiles, if you ever thought that. In other words, the Import tab is for when you want to have full control over which steps are imported.
- Create a new tab, say titled Addons. Start with a list of profiles that have upgrade steps that can be run. Then have a list of profiles that can be applied. Add the new UI options from pull request #16 here, giving control over how dependency profiles are handled. Maybe we can actually show the list of dependency profiles and give their current status: installed / not installed / needs upgrade. Do not give the option to select individual import steps: this page is for importing a full profile.

Given the new Addons tab, it probably makes sense for the Import tab to not apply or upgrade any dependency profiles. We could warn when we see this is a problem, and point to the Addons tab.

I am willing to work on this.

issue_title: Feature File Specification says UseMarkFilteringSet not supported as of 2008
issue_body:
The OpenType Feature File Specification describes the UseMarkFilteringSet flag as “a new lookup flag which is proposed for OpenType spec 1.6” and says “The drawback to this lookupflag is that as of Nov 2008, there are not yet any programs that support it.”

It’s October 2017, the current OpenType Specification version is 1.8.2, useMarkFilteringSet is part of it, and I find this flag quite well supported in HarfBuzz, CoreText, and the engine used by Edge (DirectWrite? UniScribe?). Is there any rendering system left that doesn’t support the flag?

The description should remove “new”, “proposed for OpenType spec 1.6”, and the entire last sentence, unless there are any rendering systems known not to support the flag in 2017 that need to be called out here.

issue_body: We use `self.to_json()` in `Package.save()` (see https://github.com/frictionlessdata/datapackage-py/blob/3d25cfd2013c0ddb6b5f9ceb7724dcd111fc9905/datapackage/package.py#L216), but this method is deprecated (see https://github.com/frictionlessdata/datapackage-py/blob/3d25cfd2013c0ddb6b5f9ceb7724dcd111fc9905/datapackage/package.py#L429-L438). We should either not deprecate it, or don't use it ourselves.

issue_title: Support env://<environment-variable> for the url attribute of the add_resource processor
issue_body: Like the `engine` attribute of the `dump.to_sql` processor, the `url` attribute of the `add_resource` processor might contain sensitive information like an SQL connection string when loading a table.  When publishing a pipeline, it would be nice not to have to include this information directly in the YAML.

issue_title: Make Python 2 explicitely disallowed
issue_body:
### Description

People are spending time running Ricecooker in Python 2, which doesn't work, and then they have stranger errors. Recall that both `pip` and `python` commands default to 2.7 on most systems, so it's very easy to fall into this trap.

Python 2.7 is not in the tox.ini matrix anyways.

We should throw a `RuntimeError("Cannot run this in Python 2")` in `ricecooker/__init__.py`

Previous discussion on it here:

https://github.com/learningequality/ricecooker/issues/5#issuecomment-250831114

Is there any compelling reason that Ricecooker should support Python 2.7 ?

issue_title: Rebalance is not processing after broker restart
issue_body:
In case when broker instance dies, rebalance is added to actions queue, But 'start' action forces rebalance not to process (started rebalance is removed because of start progress), ongoing rebalances are removed during other (or this) broker restart.
Root cause of this bug - 'start' and 'rebalance' actions are added to queue in the same time, (first - rebalance, then - start), then in run() rebalance says that it will stop because there is start in progress, and start just starts.

issue_title: Can't fetch the always-present bare tree 4b825dc642cb6eb9a060e54bf8d69288fbee4904
issue_body:
The empty tree `4b825dc642cb6eb9a060e54bf8d69288fbee4904` always exists, even in bare git repositories with nothing in `.git/objects`:

```bash
~/tmp/bare_git $ git show 4b825dc642cb6eb9a060e54bf8d69288fbee4904
tree 4b825dc642cb6eb9a060e54bf8d69288fbee4904

```

It's useful to have access to in GitPython for when you want to `.diff()` against a bare repo, but it doesn't always exist on disk, so GitPython can't always find it:

```python
>>> import git
>>> r = git.Repo(".")
>>> r.rev_parse("4b825dc642cb6eb9a060e54bf8d69288fbee4904")
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "<stdin>", line 1, in <module>
  File "virtualenv/lib/python2.7/site-packages/git/repo/fun.py", line 304, in rev_parse
    obj = name_to_object(repo, rev)
  File "virtualenv/lib/python2.7/site-packages/git/repo/fun.py", line 120, in name_to_object
    return Object.new_from_sha(repo, hex_to_bin(hexsha))
  File "virtualenv/lib/python2.7/site-packages/git/objects/base.py", line 65, in new_from_sha
    oinfo = repo.odb.info(sha1)
  File "virtualenv/lib/python2.7/site-packages/gitdb/db/base.py", line 205, in info
    return self._db_query(sha).info(sha)
  File "virtualenv/lib/python2.7/site-packages/gitdb/db/base.py", line 192, in _db_query
    raise BadObject(sha)
gitdb.exc.BadObject: BadObject: 4b825dc642cb6eb9a060e54bf8d69288fbee4904
```

I've been hacking around it using `git.Tree(repo, binascii.unhexlify("4b825dc642cb6eb9a060e54bf8d69288fbee4904"))` but it might be useful to have easy access to it, or a simplified way to diff against a bare repo.

issue_title: Issue with _flatten in v0.5 vs v0.3
issue_body:
Hi everyone,
  First, thanks for the great work on this library.  I noticed a small issue with the functioning of _flatten in release v0.5 compared to v0.3 having to do with the _flatten function in utilities.py.  In v0.3 I used a Pandas dataframe to feed a HeatMap object like so:

#### Please add a code sample or a nbviewer link, copy-pastable if possible

```python
import pandas as pd
import folium
from folium import plugins
pts = pd.DataFrame([[ 27.7336, -97.4218],
                    [ 41.2001, -90.7496],
                    [ 27.7522, -98.0733],
                    [ 42.0263, -93.6147],
                    [ 36.9755, -93.7223]])
m = folium.Map(location=[40.8716, -92.2727], zoom_start=4)
m.add_child(plugins.HeatMap(data=pts.values, radius = 15))
```
I get: `TypeError: only length-1 arrays can be converted to Python scalars`

This is due (I think) to the _flatten function in utilities.py only looking for lists or tuples, not arrays (as is output by Pandas `value` or `as_matrix` attribute).  This wasn't a problem in v0.3, but when I updated, it popped up as a new error.

Would it be helpful for me to fix this? Not sure if this change was by design or if I'm misunderstanding something about the error.

issue_title: Http attribute names are not consistent with the spec and some other OC implementations
issue_body:
Here are attributes that are used in python:
https://github.com/census-instrumentation/opencensus-python/blob/ae727954bd03af9db768ba44721e7436be0b768f/opencensus/trace/attributes_helper.py

```
COMMON_ATTRIBUTES = {
    'AGENT': 'g.co/agent',
    'COMPONENT': '/component',
    'ERROR_MESSAGE': '/error/message',
    'ERROR_NAME': '/error/name',
    'HTTP_CLIENT_CITY': '/http/client_city',
    'HTTP_CLIENT_COUNTRY': '/http/client_country',
    'HTTP_CLIENT_PROTOCOL': '/http/client_protocol',
    'HTTP_CLIENT_REGION': '/http/client_region',
    'HTTP_HOST': '/http/host',
    'HTTP_METHOD': '/http/method',
    'HTTP_REDIRECTED_URL': '/http/redirected_url',
    'HTTP_REQUEST_SIZE': '/http/request/size',
    'HTTP_RESPONSE_SIZE': '/http/response/size',
    'HTTP_STATUS_CODE': '/http/status_code',
    'HTTP_URL': '/http/url',
    'HTTP_USER_AGENT': '/http/user_agent',
    'PID': '/pid',
    'STACKTRACE': '/stacktrace',
    'TID': '/tid',
}
```

https://github.com/census-instrumentation/opencensus-specs/blob/master/trace/HTTP.md#attributes

suggests that http attributes should look like `http.host` rather than '/http/host' and exporter is responsible to translate them to the particular backend format.
